(In fact, the idea of sponsoring a child as a charitable hook dates back to the 1950s, when a young Christian minister encouraged Americans to sponsor needy Korean orphans.) The concept works with animals, too. At Farm Sanctuary, a nonprofit organization that fights to reduce cruel treatment of farm animals, donors can “adopt a chicken” ($10 per month), a goat ($25), or a cow ($50).

没人愿意捐款给慈善机构的行政总基金。从理智上讲，很容易理解为什么需要行政总基金——总得有人买生活必需品——但很难对办公用品产生热情。

No one wants to donate to the General Administrative Fund of a charity. It’s easy to understand, intellectually, why general funds would be needed—someone’s got to buy the staples—but it’s hard to generate a lot of passion for office supplies.

慈善机构已经学会了如何唤起捐赠者的同情和怜悯之心——值得庆幸的是，他们在这方面做得很好，因为他们的技巧减轻了许多人的痛苦。但是，“让人们关心”并非慈善机构的专属职责。管理者必须让员工足够关心，才能让他们长时间努力地完成复杂的任务。教师必须让学生关心文学。活动家必须让人们关心市议会的各项举措。

Charities have learned how to arouse sympathy and compassion in donors—and thank goodness they’re good at it, because their skills ease a lot of suffering. But “making people care” isn’t something that only charities need to do. Managers have to make people care enough to work long and hard on complex tasks. Teachers have to make students care about literature. Activists have to make people care about city council initiatives.

本章探讨了粘性的情感因素，但并非像某种手段那样去触动人们的情感按钮。电影催泪弹。更确切地说，传递“情感”信息的目的是为了引起人们的共鸣。情感能够激励人们采取行动。

This chapter tackles the emotional component of stickiness, but it’s not about pushing people’s emotional buttons, like some kind of movie tearjerker. Rather, the goal of making messages “emotional” is to make people care. Feelings inspire people to act.

举例来说，大多数青少年都认为吸烟有害健康。这个信息本身并没有可信度问题。然而，青少年仍然会吸烟。那么，如何才能将他们的信念转化为实际行动呢？你必须让他们真正关心这个问题。1998年，终于有人找到了解决办法。

As an example, most teenagers believe that cigarette smoking is dangerous. There’s no credibility problem with that message. Yet teenagers still take up smoking. So how do you transform their belief into action? You have to make them care. And, in 1998, someone finally figured out how to do that.

真相

The Truth

广告开头是纽约市的一条街道镜头。这段画面是录像，而不是胶片——画面有点暗，也略显粗糙。感觉更像是一部纪录片，而不是广告。屏幕下方闪现出一行字幕：“某大型烟草公司总部外。”

The commercial starts with a shot of a city street in New York City. The footage is video, not film—it’s a bit dark, a bit unprofessional. It feels like a documentary, not a commercial. A caption flashes at the bottom of the screen: “Outside the headquarters of a major tobacco company.”

一辆十八轮大卡车停在大楼前，一群青少年跳下车。他们开始卸下印有“尸袋”字样的白色长麻袋，并将麻袋堆放在大楼边缘。随着广告的播放，尸袋堆越来越高。广告结束时，尸袋堆已达数百个。其中一个青少年对着大楼用扩音器喊道：“你们知道烟草每天害死多少人吗？” 答案揭晓：每天的死亡人数是1800人——这正是青少年们在烟草公司总部前堆放的尸袋数量。

An eighteen-wheeler pulls up in front of the building, and a group of teenagers jump out. The teens begin to unload long white sacks marked “Body Bag.” They stack the bags on top of one another near the edge of the building. As the commercial progresses, the pile of body bags gets bigger and bigger. By the end of the ad, there are hundreds of bags in the pile. One of the teens shouts at the building through a megaphone, “Do you know how many people tobacco kills every day?” The daily death toll is revealed to be 1,800—the number of body bags the teens have piled up in front of the tobacco headquarters.

这则广告是名为“真相运动”系列广告的一部分。该运动由美国遗产基金会发起，该基金会成立于1998年11月，此前46个州的检察长与美国主要烟草公司达成和解，结束了一起诉讼。

This ad is part of a series of ads called the Truth campaign. The campaign was launched by the American Legacy Foundation, which was formed in November 1998 after forty-six state attorneys-general settled a lawsuit against major U.S. tobacco companies.

看完“真相”广告，你一定会对烟草公司感到愤怒。广告播出后，菲利普·莫里斯公司援引了烟草巨头们特有的“反诽谤”条款，要求撤下这些广告。烟草公司在和解协议中加入了这一条款。在多起反烟草诉讼中，他们拥有对和解金如何使用于反吸烟广告的否决权。菲利普·莫里斯公司负责青少年吸烟预防的高级副总裁卡罗琳·利维在谈到审查行动时表示：“我们认为（‘真相’广告）与美国遗产基金会的宗旨和使命不符。”

You can’t watch the Truth ads without getting angry at tobacco companies. After the ads began airing, Philip Morris invoked a special Big Tobacco “anti-vilification” clause to have the spots yanked from the air. The tobacco companies inserted this clause in the settlements of a number of antitobacco lawsuits; it gives them some veto power over how the settlement money can be spent on antismoking advertising. “We felt that [the Truth ads] are not consistent with the focus and mission of the American Legacy Foundation,” said Carolyn Levy, Philip Morris’s senior vice president for youth-smoking prevention, in reference to the censorship effort.

这条投诉的一种翻译是：广告效果很好。

One translation of this complaint: The ads were working.

与此同时，另一系列反吸烟广告开始播出。作为烟草和解协议的一部分，菲利普·莫里斯公司同意播出自己的一系列反吸烟广告。菲利普·莫里斯公司的广告语是“三思而后行，切勿吸烟”。

Meanwhile, another series of antismoking ads started to run. As part of the tobacco settlement, Philip Morris agreed to air its own series of antismoking ads. The Philip Morris tagline was “Think. Don’t Smoke.”

两项运动几乎同时发起，采用了两种截然不同的方法。这种并置引发了一场激动人心的思想竞赛。事实上，2002年6月，《美国公共卫生杂志》发表了一篇文章，对10692名青少年进行了调查，比较了“真相运动”和“思考，不要吸烟”运动。

Two campaigns were launched, almost simultaneously, with two different approaches. This juxtaposition set up an exciting, head-to-head horse race in the marketplace of ideas. In fact, in June 2002, an article in the American Journal of Public Health surveyed 10,692 teenagers to compare the Truth campaign with “Think. Don’t Smoke.”

事实证明，有些广告比其他广告更令人印象深刻。当被问及看过哪些反烟草广告时，22% 的孩子能自发回忆起“真相”（Truth）广告，而只有 3% 的孩子能回忆起“思考”（Think）广告。这个数据尤其引人注目：当向孩子们提供广告信息后，超过 70% 的孩子都记得看过这两个广告。换句话说，青少年们在电视上看过这两个广告，但其中一个广告的印象更深刻。“真相”广告的某些特质使其更容易被人们记住。

It turns out that some horses run better than others. When kids were asked to recall any antitobacco advertising they had seen, the Truth campaign was remembered spontaneously by 22 percent of them; the Think campaign by 3 percent. What’s particularly striking about this statistic is that when the kids were prompted with information from the campaigns, more than 70 percent of them remembered seeing both. In other words, teens had seen both ads on TV, but one stuck better than the other. Something about the Truth campaign was spontaneously memorable.

记忆固然重要，但这仅仅是第一步。行动呢？当调查询问孩子们在未来一年内是否可能吸烟时，接触过“真相”宣传活动的孩子吸烟的可能性降低了66%。而接触过“三思而后行，切勿吸烟”宣传活动的孩子吸烟的可能性则增加了36% ！烟草公司的高管们肯定很难接受这个结果。

Memory is important, but it’s only the first step. What about action? When the survey asked kids whether they were likely to smoke a cigarette during the next year, those who were exposed to the Truth campaign were 66 percent less likely to smoke. Those who were exposed to “Think. Don’t Smoke” were 36 percent more likely to smoke! Tobacco execs must have taken the news quite hard.

不仅仅是调查报告记录了这种差异。一项研究测量了佛罗里达州青少年的吸烟情况，而“真相运动”正是在那里开展的。与全国其他地区相比，该地区的吸烟率显著下降。经过两年的运动，高中生吸烟率下降了18%，初中生吸烟率下降了40%。（其中约一半的下降可能与研究期间香烟税的提高有关。）

It wasn’t just surveys that registered the difference. A study measured teen smoking in Florida, where the Truth campaign had its debut, versus the rest of the country. After two years of the campaign, smoking among high school students dropped by 18 percent and among middle school students by 40 percent. (About half of this decline may have been associated with a rise in cigarette taxes during the time of the study.)

这里发生了什么？这不就是“救助儿童会”的例子重现吗？“三思而后行，切勿吸烟”运动的核心是什么？呃，就是思考。这就是分析思维。还记得当捐赠者被要求在捐款前进行分析思考时，Rokia 的捐款情况如何吗？

What happened here? It’s the Save the Children example revisited. What is the “Think. Don’t Smoke” campaign about? Er, thinking. It’s the Analytical Hat. Remember what happened with contributions to Rokia when donors were asked to think analytically before donating?

“真相”运动的目的是什么？它旨在利用青少年特有的反权威情绪。过去，青少年吸烟是为了反抗权威。而如今，“真相”运动巧妙地将烟草巨头描绘成一个虚伪狡诈的形象，青少年反抗权威的方式也随之改变——他们不再吸烟。

What’s the Truth campaign about? It’s about tapping into anti-authority resentment, the classic teenage emotion. Once, teens smoked to rebel against The Man. Thanks to the ingenious framing of the Truth campaign—which paints a picture of a duplicitous Big Tobacco—teens now rebel against The Man by not smoking.

“真相”运动并非关乎理性决策，而是关乎反抗。它让许多青少年开始关注此事，并采取了行动。但这一次，他们什么也没做。

The Truth campaign isn’t about rational decision-making; it’s about rebellion. And it made a lot of teens care enough to do something. In this case, that something was nothing.

语义延伸与

联想的力量

Semantic Stretch and the

Power of Association

到目前为止，我们一直在讨论你可能预期的关于情感的章节内容——复杂而基本的人类情感，例如同理心（罗基亚）和愤怒（真理）。但本章的主要问题更为根本：我们如何才能让人们关注我们的信息？好消息是，要让人们关注我们的理念，我们不必凭空捏造情感。事实上，许多理念都采用了一种“搭便车”策略，将自身与已存在的情感联系起来。

So far we’ve been talking about what you might expect from a chapter on emotion—complex, fundamental human emotions like empathy (Rokia) and anger (the Truth). But the main question of this chapter is even more basic: How do we make people care about our messages? The good news is that to make people care about our ideas we don’t have to produce emotion from an absence of emotion. In fact, many ideas use a sort of piggybacking strategy, associating themselves with emotions that already exist.

请看以下影评中的一句话：“《罗生门》可以被视为爱因斯坦相对论的电影化延伸。” 《罗生门》是日本导演阿基拉于1950年执导的经典影片。黑泽明。在这部电影中，四个不同的角色从各自的视角描述了同一事件——一起谋杀和强奸案。影片采用一系列倒叙手法，每个角色依次讲述他们各自的版本。但角色的叙述都带有自我辩解的色彩，前后矛盾，影片结束时，观众仍然无法确定真相究竟如何。这部电影质疑了绝对真理的存在——或者至少质疑了我们探寻真理的能力。

Consider the following sentence from a movie review: “Rashomon can be seen as a cinematic extension of Einstein’s theory of relativity.” Rashomon is a classic 1950 film by the Japanese director Akira Kurosawa. In the film, four different characters describe the same event—a murder and rape—from their own perspectives. The movie is told in a series of flashbacks, as each of the characters recounts his or her version of events. But the characters’ tales are self-serving and contradictory, and at the end of the movie the viewer is still uncertain about what actually happened. The movie questions the existence of absolute truth—or, at least, our ability to uncover it.

所以，上面引述的影评人是将《罗生门》中的“相对真理”与爱因斯坦的相对论相比较。但爱因斯坦的相对论并非旨在说明“一切皆相对”。事实上，它的真正含义恰恰相反。该理论旨在解释物理定律在所有参考系中都相同。从爱因斯坦的角度来看，事物并非难以预测，而是出奇地井然有序。

So the movie reviewer, in the quote above, was comparing Rashomon’s “relative truth” to Einstein’s theory of relativity. But Einstein’s theory of relativity wasn’t designed to say that “everything is relative.” In fact, its actual meaning was essentially the opposite. The theory was designed to explain how the laws of physics are identical in every frame of reference. From Einstein’s view, things don’t look unpredictable; they look surprisingly orderly.

为什么评论者将《罗生门》与相对论联系起来？这种联系似乎并非诉诸爱因斯坦的权威，而是声称《罗生门》是爱因斯坦理论的电影“等价物”。相反，这种类比似乎意在营造一种敬畏之感——它暗示，当我们观看《罗生门》时，我们将面对某种深刻的事物。

Why did the reviewer link Rashomon with relativity? This reference doesn’t look like an appeal to Einstein’s authority; it claims that Rashomon is the cinematic “equivalent” of Einstein’s theory. Instead, the analogy seems intended to create a sense of awe—when we watch Rashomon, it implies, we will be in the presence of something profound.

之所以借用相对论，是因为它能为这部电影增添一种情感共鸣——深刻、敬畏。上面的影评只是成千上万个例子中的一个。“相对论”在某种意义上成为了概念调色板上的一种颜色。当你想要唤起敬畏之情时，你只需在“相对论”上轻轻点缀。其他科学术语——“不确定性原理”、“混沌理论”、“量子力学的量子跃迁”——也都是这个调色板上的颜色。

The theory of relativity is borrowed, as an association, because it lends an aura of emotional resonance—profundity, awe—to the movie. The movie review above is just one example among thousands. “Relativity” becomes, in a sense, a color on the idea palette. When you want to conjure up awe, you dab your brush into “relativity.” Other scientific terms—the “uncertainty principle,” “chaos theory,” the “quantum leap” of quantum mechanics—are also colors on this palette.

1929年，爱因斯坦抗议道：“哲学家们玩弄文字，就像孩子玩弄玩偶一样……这并不意味着生活中的一切都是相对的。”令爱因斯坦懊恼的是，试图理解“相对论”的人数开始超过试图理解相对论的人数。

In 1929, Einstein protested, “Philosophers play with the word, like a child with a doll…. It does not mean that everything in life is relative.” To Einstein’s chagrin, the number of people trying to tap into the resonance of “relativity” began to exceed the number of people who were trying to understand relativity.

当某些术语被反复联想——有时精准，有时粗糙——其结果就是削弱了这些术语及其潜在概念的力量。当每个人都用石灰绿作画时，石灰绿就不再引人注目了。

When associations to certain terms are drawn repeatedly—sometimes with precision, sometimes with crudeness—the effect is to dilute the power of the terms and their underlying concepts. When everyone paints with lime green, lime green no longer stands out.

斯坦福大学和耶鲁大学的研究表明，这种利用词语和概念的情感联想来达到沟通目的的做法是常见的现象。人们往往会过度使用任何能引发情感共鸣的想法或概念。这项研究将这种过度使用现象称为“语义延伸”。

Research conducted at Stanford and Yale shows that this process—exploiting terms and concepts for their emotional associations—is a common characteristic of communication. People tend to overuse any idea or concept that delivers an emotional kick. The research labeled this overuse “semantic stretch.”

我们来看一个非科学方面的例子：“独特”这个词。“独特”过去指的是独一无二的。“独特”意味着特别。

Let’s look at a nonscientific example: the word “unique.” “Unique” used to mean one of a kind. “Unique” was special.

研究人员利用数据库，分析了美国排名前五十的报纸在过去二十年间发表的每一篇文章。在此期间，将某事物描述为“独特”的文章比例增加了73%。因此，要么是当今世界独特的事物数量大幅增加，要么是“独特性”的标准降低了。

The researchers used a database to examine every newspaper article in each of the top fifty newspapers in the United States over a twenty-year period. During this time, the percentage of articles in which something was described as “unique” increased by 73 percent. So either there’s a lot more unique stuff in the world today or the “uniqueness bar” has been lowered.

或许有些持怀疑态度的人，比如想到扫地机器人或帕丽斯·希尔顿，会反驳说：“嘿，现在世界上有很多更独特的东西。” 但与此同时，“独特”一词的使用频率却在下降。1985年，文章中使用“不寻常”一词的概率是使用“独特”一词的两倍多。到了2005年，这两个词的使用频率几乎持平。

Perhaps some skeptics, contemplating robot vacuum cleaners or Paris Hilton, would protest, “Hey, there is a lot more unique stuff in the world these days.” But at the same time that the word “unique” was rising in popularity, the word “unusual” was falling. In 1985, articles were more than twice as likely to use the word “unusual” as the word “unique.” By 2005, the two words were about equally likely to be used.

独特的事物应该是不寻常事物的一个子集——独特（即独一无二）本身就非常不寻常。因此，如果如今独特的事物真的更多了，我们也应该看到更多“不寻常”的事物。然而，不寻常的事物越来越少，这使得独特事物的兴起看起来像是语义上的延伸。我们过去称之为“不寻常”的东西，现在被我们延伸并称为“独特”。

Unique things should be a subset of unusual things—unique (i.e., one of a kind) is about as unusual as you can get. So if there really were more unique things today, we should see more “unusual” things as well. The fact that unusual things are getting less common makes the rise in unique things look like a case of semantic stretch. What we used to call “unusual” we now stretch and call “unique.”

那么，“相对性”和“独特性”这两个词的情感意义在哪里呢？关键在于：让人们关心某件事的最基本方法，就是在他们原本不关心的事物和他们关心的事物之间建立联系。我们所有人都会自然而然地运用这种策略。联想。“相对性”和“独特性”告诉我们，在使用联想时，我们可能会过度使用颜色。随着时间的推移，联想会被过度使用，其价值也会被稀释；人们最终会说“这真是独一无二”。

So where’s the emotion in “relativity” and in “unique”? Here’s the punch line: The most basic way to make people care is to form an association between something they don’t yet care about and something they do care about. We all naturally practice the tactic of association. What “relativity” and “unique” teach us is that in using associations we can overuse colors. Over time, associations get overused and become diluted in value; people end up saying things like “This is really, truly unique.”

一代人最爱的形容词——酷炫、惊艳、帅气、时髦——会随着时间流逝而褪色，因为它们被赋予了太多含义。当你听到父亲用“酷”来形容某样东西时，“酷”这个词就失去了它原本的冲击力。当你的金融学教授开始用“老兄”这个词时，你必须把它从你的词汇表中剔除。因此，运用联想就像一场军备竞赛。别人造了一枚导弹，你就得造两枚。如果他“独一无二”，你就得“超级独一无二”。

The superlatives of one generation—groovy, awesome, cool, phat—fade over time because they’ve been associated with too many things. When you hear your father call something “cool,” coolness loses its punch. When your finance professor starts using the word “dude,” you must eliminate the word from your vocabulary. Using associations, then, is an arms race of sorts. The other guy builds a missile, so you have to build two. If he’s “unique,” you’ve got to be “super-unique.”

这种情感联想的军备竞赛给那些试图引起他人共鸣的人带来了麻烦。事实上，正如我们将看到的，这种军备竞赛实际上已经让“体育精神”一词名存实亡。

This emotional-association arms race creates problems for people who are trying to make others care. In fact, as we’ll see, the arms race essentially bankrupted the term “sportsmanship.”

对抗语义延伸：

以“体育精神”为例

Fighting Semantic Stretch:

The Case of “Sportsmanship”

上一章我们讨论了积极教练联盟（PCA）创始人吉姆·汤普森举办的教练研讨会。自1988年创立PCA以来，汤普森一直致力于解决一个重要问题：如何纠正青少年体育运动中常见的不良行为？在解决这个问题的过程中，汤普森不得不面对语义延伸的问题。

In the last chapter, we discussed the coaching seminars held by Jim Thompson, the founder of the Positive Coaching Alliance (PCA). Since 1988, when he founded the PCA, Thompson has struggled with an important problem. How do you clean up the bad behavior often associated with youth sports? In grappling with this problem, Thompson had to confront the issue of semantic stretch.

网球运动员约翰·麦肯罗曾是体育精神缺失的典型代表，他经常摔球拍，还与裁判发生争执。但如今，麦肯罗的行为在许多青少年体育比赛中却不足为奇。不良行为不仅在运动员中普遍存在，在家长和其他观众中也屡见不鲜。据美国青少年体育联盟统计，近15%的青少年体育比赛中都出现了家长或教练与裁判之间的冲突，而几年前这一比例仅为5%。

The tennis player John McEnroe was once the poster child of poor sportsmanship, with his racket-throwing and bratty arguments with officials. But today McEnroe’s behavior wouldn’t raise an eyebrow at many youth sports games. Bad behavior is now common not only among athletes but also among parents and other spectators. According to the National Alliance for Youth Sports, nearly 15 percent of youth sports games involved a confrontation between parents or coaches and officials, up from 5 percent a few years ago.

体育精神曾经是体育运动中一个强有力的理念，但汤普森认为它现在已经失去了意义。“体育精神奖杯被视为给失败者的安慰奖，”他说。一位女士告诉汤普森，她的高中篮球教练曾说过，如果他的队员赢得体育精神奖杯，他们就得跑圈。汤普森补充道：“体育精神似乎主要就是不做坏事：‘不要对裁判大喊大叫。不要违反规则。’但仅仅拒绝做坏事是不够的。我们需要对青少年体育运动的参与者提出更高的要求。不幸的是，‘做一个有风度的人！’并不是我们改变青少年体育运动所需要的口号。”

Sportsmanship was once a powerful idea in athletics, but Thompson felt that it had become a weak term. “Sportsmanship trophies are seen as consolation prizes for losers,” he says. One woman told Thompson that her high school basketball coach said that if his players ever won a sportsmanship trophy, they’d have to run laps. Thompson adds, “Sportsmanship seems like it is mostly about not doing something bad: ‘Don’t yell at officials. Don’t break the rules.’ But it’s not enough to simply refuse to do bad things. We need to expect much more of participants in youth sports. Unfortunately, ‘Be a good sport!’ is not the rallying cry that we need to transform youth sports.”

每个人都喜欢听体育精神的真实例子。汤普森举了兰斯·阿姆斯特朗的例子。在环法自行车赛中，他的主要竞争对手之一扬·乌尔里希摔车后，阿姆斯特朗的反应出人意料。他没有利用这个机会扩大领先优势，而是放慢速度，等待乌尔里希重新上车。他后来表示，与乌尔里希这样的优秀运动员同场竞技时，他的发挥反而更好。这就是体育精神。

Everyone enjoys hearing about real examples of good sportsmanship. Thompson uses the example of Lance Armstrong, who reacted unexpectedly when one of his chief opponents, Jan Ullrich, crashed during the Tour de France. Instead of taking advantage of this lucky break to increase his lead, Armstrong slowed down and waited for Ullrich to remount. He later said that he rode better when he was competing with a great athlete like Ullrich. That’s sportsmanship.

汤普森知道，人们仍然敬佩体育精神的根本理念。家长们希望孩子们能从体育运动中学习尊重和礼仪。教练们希望成为孩子们的良师益友，而不仅仅是胜利的监工。孩子们也希望自己的队伍能受到他人的尊重。这三方有时都会犯错，表现得像个混蛋。但汤普森看到，即使“体育精神”这个词本身已经失去了激励良好行为的力量，人们对体育精神的需求和渴望依然存在。

Thompson knew that people still admired the underlying ideals of sportsmanship. Parents did want their kids to learn respect and manners from athletics. Coaches did want to be mentors, not just victorious taskmasters. Kids did want their teams to be respected by others. All three groups sometimes slipped up and acted like jerks. But Thompson saw that the need and the desire for sportsmanship remained, even though the term “sportsmanship” had lost its ability to motivate good behavior.

“体育精神”一词的含义被过度延伸了。就像“相对论”一样，它早已偏离了最初的含义。它原本指的是兰斯·阿姆斯特朗对扬·乌尔里希展现的那种行为。但随着时间的推移，这个词的含义被扩大到包括一些令人不齿、缺乏骑士精神的行为，比如输球后不抱怨太多，或者在整场比赛中不攻击裁判。

“Sportsmanship” had been stretched too far. Like “relativity,” it had migrated far afield from its original meaning. It used to refer to the kind of behavior that Lance Armstrong showed Jan Ullrich. But over time the term was stretched to include unimpressive, nonchivalrous behavior, like losing without whining too much or making it through an entire game without assaulting a referee.

汤普森和PCA需要一种不同的方式来鼓励人们，不仅要避免不良行为，还要拥抱良好行为。

Thompson and the PCA needed a different way of encouraging people, not just to avoid bad behavior but to embrace good behavior.

他们称之为“尊重比赛”。人们关心体育，他们关心比赛本身。这是一种强调比赛及其完整性高于个人参与者的方式。“尊重比赛”是一种体育爱国主义。它意味着你对你所从事的运动负有基本的尊重。阿姆斯特朗并非“展现体育精神”，而是在“尊重比赛”。“尊重比赛”不仅适用于运动员，也适用于其他人。它提醒所有人，体育是一项公共事业。破坏这项事业是不体面的，也是不光彩的。

They called it Honoring the Game. People care about sports, they care about the Game. It’s a way of making the point that the Game and its integrity are larger than the individual participants. “Honoring the Game” is a kind of sports patriotism. It implies that you owe your sport basic respect. Armstrong wasn’t being a “good sport;” he was Honoring the Game. And Honoring the Game also works for people other than players. It reminds anyone that sports is a civic institution. It’s unseemly to mess with an institution. It’s dishonorable.

是否有证据表明“尊重比赛”有效？不妨看看德克萨斯州达拉斯市一个篮球联盟收集的数据：“在2002赛季，平均每15场比赛就会出现一次技术犯规。自那以后，我们举办了六次‘双目标教练’培训。到了2004赛季，平均每52场比赛才会出现一次技术犯规。”北加州的一个棒球联盟发现，在接受积极教练培训后，因不良行为而被驱逐出场的球员人数大幅减少（减少了90%！）。球队士气也显著提升，联盟的球员人数增加了20%。唯一的抱怨是场地不够用了。

Is there any proof that Honoring the Game works? Consider the data gathered by a basketball league in Dallas, Texas: “In the 2002 basketball season, on average there was a technical foul called every fifteen games. Since that time, we’ve conducted six Double-Goal Coach workshops. In the 2004 basketball season, there was a technical foul called every fifty-two games.” A baseball league in Northern California found that after Positive Coaching training, there was a dramatic reduction (90 percent!) in the number of people who were ejected from games for bad behavior. Team morale improved so much that the number of players enrolling in the league increased by 20 percent. The only complaint was that they were running out of fields.

汤普森想要改变的不仅仅是青少年体育运动的文化，他想要改变所有体育运动的文化：“我有一个设想。我正在观看世界大赛，一位教练冲进场内，斥责一位他不认同判罚的裁判。在全国电视直播中，鲍勃·科斯塔斯说道：‘看到这位教练以这种方式玷污棒球运动，真是太糟糕了。’”（顺便提一句，请注意这个设想多么具体。）

Thompson doesn’t want to change just the culture of youth sports. He wants to change the culture of all sports: “I have a fantasy. I’m watching the World Series and a manager comes rushing onto the field to berate an umpire who made a call he disagrees with. On national TV, Bob Costas says, ‘That’s really too bad to see the manager dishonoring the game of baseball that way.’” (As a side note, notice how wonderfully concrete this vision is.)

青少年体育运动中仍然存在着不礼貌的现象，但汤普森在他所到之处确实带来了切实的改变。而且，他提出的“尊重比赛”理念，巧妙地避开了语义上的牵强附会，真正触动了人们的内心。

Youth sports hasn’t been purged of discourtesy, but Thompson is making a tangible difference in the places he’s reached. And, with Honoring the Game, he has managed to sidestep semantic stretch and peg an idea that makes people care.

对我们其他人来说，这说明如果我们想让人们关心某件事，就必须抓住他们关心的事情。当每个人都关注某件事时，事情就会变得无关紧要。如果大家都陷入同样的​​境地，就会出现军备竞赛。为了避免这种情况，我们要么像汤普森那样开拓新的领域，要么找到能够体现我们理念的独特协会。

The lesson for the rest of us is that if we want to make people care, we’ve got to tap into the things they care about. When everybody taps into the same thing, an arms race emerges. To avoid it, we’ve either got to shift onto new turf, as Thompson did, or find associations that are distinctive for our ideas.

诉诸自身利益

Appealing to Self-Interest

我们正在寻找方法，让人们关注我们的理念——关注非洲儿童罗基亚的遭遇、关注吸烟问题、关注慈善事业、关注体育精神。我们通过诉诸人们关心的事情来赢得他们的关注。

We’re searching for ways to make people care about our ideas—to make them care about the African child Rokia, about smoking, about charity, about sportsmanship. We make people care by appealing to the things that matter to them.

那么，人们真正关心的是什么呢？到目前为止，我们讨论的都是人际关系，但其实还有一个更直接的答案。事实上，这或许是最显而易见的答案。人们真正关心的是什么？人们最关心的是自己。因此，让人们关心自身利益是让他们产生共鸣的有效方法之一，这一点不足为奇。

And what matters to people? So far, we’ve dealt with associations, but there’s a more direct answer. In fact, it might be the most obvious answer of all. What matters to people? People matter to themselves. It will come as no surprise that one reliable way of making people care is by invoking self-interest.

1925年，约翰·卡普尔斯受命为美国音乐学院的函授音乐课程广告撰写标题。卡普尔斯此前并无广告经验，但他却天赋异禀。他坐在打字机前，敲出了印刷广告史上最著名的标题：“当我坐在钢琴前时，他们都嘲笑我……但当我开始弹奏时，他们却嘲笑我！”

In 1925, John Caples was assigned to write a headline for an advertisement promoting the correspondence music course offered by the U.S. School of Music. Caples had no advertising experience, but he was a natural. He sat at his typewriter and pecked out the most famous headline in print-advertising history: “They Laughed When I Sat Down at the Piano … But When I Started to Play!”

这是一个经典的逆袭故事，用十五个字概括：人们嘲笑他！但他用琴声让他们闭嘴！（标题足够吸引人，让我们忽略了一些常识性的反应，比如：呃，为什么会有人嘲笑坐在钢琴前的人？你上次嘲笑坐在钢琴前的人是什么时候？）

This is a classic underdog story in fifteen words. People laughed at him! And he shut them up through his playing! (The headline is enthralling enough that it makes us overlook commonsense reactions like, Um, why would anyone laugh at someone sitting down at a piano? When was the last time you laughed at someone who sat down at a piano?)

这个标题在函授课程的销售上取得了巨大成功，以至于几十年后仍然被文案撰稿人竞相模仿。六十年后，以下这个模仿标题使销量比上一年增长了26%：“我通过邮购订购地毯时，我丈夫笑了。但当我省了50%时……”（我们的出版商拒绝了本书的以下副标题：“他们笑了……”）当我们写这本书的时候。但是当他们在装满冰块的浴缸里醒来时……”）

The headline was so successful at selling correspondence courses that it’s still being ripped off by copywriters decades later. Sixty years later, the following knockoff headline increased sales by 26 percent over the previous year: “My Husband Laughed When I Ordered Our Carpet Through the Mail. But When I Saved 50% …” (Our publisher rejected the following subtitle for this book: “They Laughed When We Wrote This Book. But When They Woke Up in an Ice-Filled Bathtub …”)

卡普尔斯帮助建立了邮购广告，这是现代电视购物广告的前身。与其他大多数广告形式不同，邮购广告的广告商可以精确地知道广告的效果。假设报纸或杂志上刊登了一则“选股指南”的广告。如果您想订购这本指南，就把支票寄到广告上列出的地址。但是，每个版本的广告所列的地址都略有不同，因此，当您的订单寄到某个特定地址时，营销人员就能准确地知道是哪个广告促成了这笔交易。

Caples helped establish mail-order advertising, the forerunner of the modern infomercial. In mail-order advertising, unlike most other forms of advertising, advertisers know exactly how well an ad works. Say there’s an ad for a “stock-picking guide” in a newspaper or a magazine. If you want to order the stock-picking guide, you send off a check to the address listed in the ad. But each version of an ad lists a slightly different address, so when your order shows up at a particular address the marketer knows precisely which ad generated the sale.

对比一下邮购广告和经典消费品佳洁士牙膏。人们为什么会买一支佳洁士牙膏？是因为新的电视广告？还是因为零售价优惠？还是因为包装设计新颖？还是因为妈妈一直用佳洁士？又或者是那天店里只有佳洁士这一个牌子？令人惊讶的是，营销人员对此几乎一无所知。

Contrast mail-order ads with a classic consumer product like Crest. Why does someone buy a tube of Crest? Is it because of the new TV ads? Or was it the discount price at retail? Or the fancy new package design? Or the fact that Mom always used Crest? Or that it was the only brand in stock that day? Marketers have surprisingly little ability to tell.

由于邮购广告的透明度极高，它本质上就是一个评估激励性诉求的实验室。究竟是什么让人们在意？不妨问问直邮广告文案撰写人。约翰·卡普尔斯常被誉为史上最伟大的文案撰写人。他说：“首先，也是最重要的一点，要让你的每一个标题都体现出自身利益。让你的标题暗示读者，这里有他们想要的东西。这条规则如此基本，似乎显而易见。然而，每天都有无数的文案撰写人违反它。”

Because mail-order advertising is so transparent, it’s essentially a laboratory for assessing motivational appeals. What makes people care? Ask a direct-mail copywriter. And John Caples is often cited as the greatest copywriter of all time. He says, “First and foremost, try to get self-interest into every headline you write. Make your headline suggest to readers that here is something they want. This rule is so fundamental that it would seem obvious. Yet the rule is violated every day by scores of writers.”

Caples的广告标题通过承诺以微不足道的成本获得巨大的收益，来暴露自身利益：

Caples’s ads get self-interest into their headlines by promising huge benefits for trivial costs:

  • 如果你遵循这个简单的计划，你就可以嘲笑金钱方面的担忧了。

  • You Can Laugh at Money Worries if You Follow This Simple Plan

  • 给我五天时间，我会让你拥有迷人的个性……让我来证明——免费

  • Give Me 5 Days and I’ll Give You a Magnetic Personality … Let Me Prove It—Free

  • 如何长高的秘密

  • The Secret of How to Be Taller

  • 如何利用一个晚上提升记忆力

  • How You Can Improve Your Memory in One Evening

  • 55岁退休

  • Retire at 55

卡普尔斯说，企业常常过分强调产品特性，而忽略了其真正应该强调的益处。“广告失败最常见的原因是广告商过于自夸（比如‘世界上最好的种子！’），却忘记告诉我们为什么要购买（比如‘世界上最好的草坪！’）。”一条古老的广告格言说，你必须把益处的益处说清楚。换句话说，人们购买的不是四分之一英寸的钻头，而是四分之一英寸的孔，这样他们就可以挂孩子的照片了。

Caples says companies often emphasize features when they should be emphasizing benefits. “The most frequent reason for unsuccessful advertising is advertisers who are so full of their own accomplishments (the world’s best seed!) that they forget to tell us why we should buy (the world’s best lawn!).” An old advertising maxim says you’ve got to spell out the benefit of the benefit. In other words, people don’t buy quarter-inch drill bits. They buy quarter-inch holes so they can hang their children’s pictures.

看到卡普尔斯的作品，我们感到很不舒服：他的许多广告都暗藏玄机，具有欺骗性。“磁性人格套装”的生产商或许可以逍遥法外，但我们大多数人都渴望与真相建立联系。

We get uncomfortable looking at Caples’s handiwork: Many of his ads are shady. Deceptive. The manufacturers of the Magnetic Personality Kit may enjoy a conscience-free existence, but most of us aspire to a working relationship with the truth.

那么，卡普尔斯演讲技巧中不带广告噱头、不哗众取宠的精髓是什么呢？第一课就是不要忽视自身利益。杰瑞·韦斯曼曾是电视制片人和编剧，现在指导CEO们如何发表演讲。他表示，你不应该回避诉诸自身利益。他认为，WIIFY——“对你有什么好处”（发音为“ whiff-y”） ——应该是每次演讲的核心。

So what’s the nonadvertising, nonschlocky takeaway from the Caples techniques? The first lesson is not to overlook self-interest. Jerry Weissman, a former TV producer and screenwriter who now coaches CEOs in how to deliver speeches, says that you shouldn’t dance around the appeal to self-interest. He says that the WIIFY—“what’s in it for you,” pronounced whiff-y—should be a central aspect of every speech.

韦斯曼指出，有些人不愿意把信息说得这么清楚。“但我的听众并不傻，”他引用那些反对者的话，“如果我把意思解释得这么清楚，他们甚至可能会觉得受到了侮辱！” 然而，对于可能注意力分散的听众来说，把信息解释清楚是有价值的：“即使他们只需要几秒钟就能把你描述的功能和隐含的好处联系起来，等他们反应过来的时候，你已经讲到下一个要点了，他们可能就没有时间去理解这个好处……或者下一个要点了。”

Weissman notes that some people resist spelling out the message. “But my audiences aren’t stupid,” he quotes the resisters. “They might even feel insulted if I spell it out for them!” For an audience that may be distracted, though, spelling it out has value: “Even if it takes them just a few seconds to connect the dots between the feature you describe and the implied benefit, by the time they catch up, you will have moved on to your next point, and they probably won’t have time to absorb the benefit … or the next point.”

老师们对学生经常问的那句话“我们以后怎么用这个？”再说一遍，就是“这对我们有什么好处？”如果代数学能让学生在电子游戏方面表现更出色，会有老师犹豫地说出来吗？会有老师怀疑学生会因此更加专注吗？

Teachers are all too familiar with the student refrain “How are we ever going to use this?” In other words, what’s in it for me? If the WIIFY was that algebra made students better at video games, would any teacher hesitate to say so? Does any teacher doubt that students would pay more attention?

如果你的出发点是自身利益，那就不要隐瞒，不要拐弯抹角。即使是细微的调整也能产生影响。卡普尔斯说，重要的是要让“自身利益”始终贯穿其中：“不要说，‘人们使用固特异轮胎会感到安全’，而要说，‘你使用固特异轮胎会感到安全’。”

If you’ve got self-interest on your side, don’t bury it. Don’t talk around it. Even subtle tweaks can make a difference. It’s important, Caples says, to keep the self in self-interest: “Don’t say, ‘People will enjoy a sense of security when they use Goodyear Tires.’ Say, ‘You enjoy a sense of security when you use Goodyear Tires.’”

当然，除了邮购广告所宣传的那些令人反感、不那么露骨的迎合自身利益的方式之外，还有许多其他方式。为了探究这一点，我们将从一项在亚利桑那州坦佩市进行的颇为奇特的研究开始。

Of course, there are less obnoxious, less overt ways to appeal to self-interest than those promoted by mail-order ads. To explore this, we’ll start with a rather odd study conducted in Tempe, Arizona.

坦佩的有线电视

Cable TV in Tempe

1982年，心理学家在亚利桑那州坦佩市对一群房主进行了一项关于说服的研究。学生志愿者拜访了这些房主，请他们填写调查问卷，作为一项课堂项目的一部分。

In 1982, psychologists conducted a study on persuasion with a group of homeowners in Tempe, Arizona. The homeowners were visited by student volunteers who asked them to fill out surveys for a class project.

当时，有线电视刚刚兴起，对大多数人来说还很陌生。这项研究旨在比较两种不同的方法在向房主普及有线电视潜在益处方面的成效。

At the time, cable TV was just starting to appear—it was still unfamiliar to most people. The research study was designed to compare the success of two different approaches to educating the homeowners about the potential benefits of cable TV.

一组房主被告知，有线电视可能物有所值：

One group of homeowners was presented with some information about why cable might be worthwhile:

有线电视将为用户提供更广泛的娱乐和资讯服务。如果使用得当，用户可以提前计划，尽情享受各种活动。这样一来，就无需花费保姆费和油费，也无需忍受外出带来的种种不便，就能有更多时间待在家中，与家人、朋友或家人共度美好时光。

CATV will provide a broader entertainment and informational service to its subscribers. Used properly, a person can plan in advance to enjoy events offered. Instead of spending money on the babysitter and gas, and putting up with the hassles of going out, more time can be spent at home with family, alone, or with friends.

第二组房主被要求想象自己身处一个详细的场景中：

The second group of homeowners was asked to imagine themselves in a detailed scenario:

花点时间想象一下有线电视将如何为您提供更丰富的娱乐和资讯服务。合理使用有线电视，您可以提前计划想要观看的节目。想想看，您无需再花钱请保姆、加油，也无需忍受出门的种种不便，就能把时间花在家里，陪伴家人、享受独处时光，或者与朋友欢聚。

Take a moment and imagine how CATV will provide you with a broader entertainment and informational service. When you use it properly, you will be able to plan in advance which of the events offered you wish to enjoy. Take a moment and think of how, instead of spending money on the babysitter and gas, and then having to put up with the hassles of going out, you will be able to spend your time at home, with your family, alone, or with your friends.

有些读者表示，起初他们并没有看出这两篇诉求之间的区别。区别确实很细微。但请回过头来，数一数每篇诉求中“你”这个词出现的次数。

Some readers have said that at first they didn’t see any difference between the two appeals. The difference is subtle. But go back and count up the number of times the word “you” appears in each appeal.

从某种意义上说，这项研究是对卡普尔斯建议的更详细阐述，即避免谈论抽象的益处（例如“人们使用固特异轮胎会感到安全”），而应关注个人益处（例如“使用固特异轮胎会让你感到安全”）。然而，亚利桑那州的这项研究更进一步，它要求参与者想象使用固特异轮胎后所获得的安全感。

In a sense, the study was a more elaborate version of Caples’s advice to avoid talking about abstract benefits (“People will enjoy a sense of security when they use Goodyear Tires”) and focus on personal benefits (“You enjoy a sense of security when you use Goodyear Tires”). The Arizona study, though, took it a step further. It asked people to visualize the feeling of security they would get by using Goodyear tires.

房主们填写完学生问卷后告别。他们以为研究项目已经结束，但研究人员还有另一个阶段的工作要做。调查结束后一个月，有线电视进入了坦佩市。当地的有线电视公司开始联系房主推销订阅服务。大学研究人员设法从有线电视公司获得了用户数据。然后，他们分析了哪些房主订阅了服务，哪些房主没有订阅。

The homeowners filled out a questionnaire for the students and said goodbye. They thought they were finished with the research project, but the researchers still had another stage to complete. A month after the survey was conducted, cable TV arrived in Tempe. The local cable company approached the homeowners for subscriptions. The university researchers managed to get subscriber data from the cable company. They then analyzed which homeowners had subscribed and which hadn’t.

那些了解有线电视信息的房主中，20%的人最终订阅了有线电视，这与附近其他房主的订阅率大致相同。但那些想象自己订阅有线电视的房主，实际订阅率却高达47%。这项研究论文发表时的副标题是“想象真的能成真吗？”答案是肯定的。

The homeowners who got information about cable subscribed at a rate of 20 percent, which was about the same as the rest of the neighborhood. But the homeowners who imagined themselves subscribing to cable subscribed at a rate of 47 percent. The research paper, when it was published, was subtitled “Does Imagining Make It So?” The answer was yes.

与典型的邮购广告相比，“想象一下有线电视”的诉求是一种更为巧妙的利己主义。值得注意的是，它所提供的好处并非像卡普尔斯（Caples）那样夸大其词。其核心在于，订购有线电视可以让你免去出门的麻烦（！）。事实上，仅仅抽象地描述这些好处并不足以吸引更多用户。只有当人们把自己代入其中——“我可以想象自己和老公在家看电影，随时可以起身去隔壁房间看看孩子……想想我能省下多少钱请保姆！” ——他们的兴趣才会真正被激发。

Compared with a typical mail-order ad, the “imagine cable television” appeal is a much more subtle appeal to self-interest. Note that the benefits offered were not fantastic in a Caples-esque way. The gist was that you could avoid the hassle of leaving home (!) by ordering cable. Indeed, just hearing about the benefits, in the abstract, wasn’t enough to lure additional subscribers. It was only when people put themselves in the starring role—I can see myself watching a good movie at home with my hubby, and I can get up and check on the kids in the next room whenever I like … and think of all that babysitting money I’d save!—that their interest grew.

这一发现表明，真正让人们在意的或许是利益的切实性，而非其规模。你不必承诺财富、性感魅力和迷人个性，只需承诺一些人们能够轻易想象自己享受到的合理利益就足够了。

This finding suggests that it may be the tangibility, rather than the magnitude, of the benefits that makes people care. You don’t have to promise riches and sex appeal and magnetic personalities. It may be enough to promise reasonable benefits that people can easily imagine themselves enjoying.

想象一下，如果救助儿童会把这个想法融入到他们的赞助宣传中会怎样？目前他们的宣传语是“您可以每月捐助30美元，资助马里的小女孩罗基娅”——这已经很成功了。但如果宣传语扩展一下呢？“想象一下，您是马里小女孩罗基娅的资助人。您的办公桌上放着她的照片，就在您孩子照片的旁边。过去一年里，您和她互通了三次信，从信中您了解到她喜欢读书，而且经常被弟弟惹恼。她很兴奋明年就能加入足球队了。” 这很有感染力。（而且一点也不粗俗。）

Imagine that Save the Children incorporated this idea into its pitches for sponsorship. Right now the pitch is “You can sponsor Rokia, a little girl in Mali, for $30 per month”—a pitch that is already successful. But what if the pitch was expanded? “Imagine yourself as the sponsor of Rokia, a little girl in Mali. You’ve got a picture of her on your desk at work, next to your kids’ pictures. During the past year you’ve traded letters with her three times, and you know from the letters that she loves to read and frequently gets annoyed by her little brother. She is excited that next year she’ll get to play on the soccer team.” That’s powerful. (And it’s not crass.)

马斯洛

Maslow

当然，自利并非全部真相——尤其当我们像通常那样，将“自利”狭义地定义为财富和安全时。如果自利就是全部真相，那就没人会参军了。人们关心的事情有很多，但这些事情永远不会出现在卡普尔斯的广告里。

Self-interest isn’t the whole story, of course—especially if we define “self-interest” narrowly, as we often do, in terms of wealth and security. If it were the whole story, no one would ever serve in the armed forces. There are things people care about that would never appear in a Caples ad.

1954年，一位名叫亚伯拉罕·马斯洛的心理学家对心理学中关于人类动机的研究进行了综述。他将大量的现有研究提炼成人们试图满足的需求和欲望清单：

In 1954, a psychologist named Abraham Maslow surveyed the research in psychology about what motivates people. He boiled down volumes of existing research to a list of needs and desires that people try to fulfill:

  • 超越：帮助他人发挥潜能

  • Transcendence: help others realize their potential

  • 自我实现：发挥自身潜能，实现自我价值，达到人生巅峰

  • Self-actualization: realize our own potential, self-fulfillment, peak experiences

  • 美学：对称、秩序、美、平衡

  • Aesthetic: symmetry, order, beauty, balance

  • 学习：了解、理解、建立联系

  • Learning: know, understand, mentally connect

  • 尊重：成就、胜​​任、获得认可、独立、地位

  • Esteem: achieve, be competent, gain approval, independence, status

  • 归属感：爱、家庭、朋友、感情

  • Belonging: love, family, friends, affection

  • 安全：保护、保障、稳定

  • Security: protection, safety, stability

  • 生理需求：饥饿、口渴、身体舒适感

  • Physical: hunger, thirst, bodily comfort

你或许记得这份清单是马斯洛需求层次理论，或者马斯洛需求层次理论。马斯洛的需求清单极具洞察力，但他将其描述为“层次结构”并不准确。马斯洛将这种层次结构视为一架梯子——需要从下往上逐级攀登。只有满足了安全感，才能满足尊重感；只有满足了生理需求，才能满足审美需求。（在马斯洛的世界里，没有挨饿的艺术家。）

You may remember this list as Maslow’s Pyramid, or Maslow’s Hierarchy of Needs. Maslow’s list of needs was incredibly insightful, but he was wrong to describe it as a “hierarchy.” Maslow saw the hierarchy as a ladder—to be climbed rung by rung from the bottom up. You couldn’t fill your longing for Esteem until you satisfied your longing for Security. You couldn’t fill your Aesthetic needs until your Physical needs were taken care of. (In Maslow’s world, there were no starving artists.)

后续研究表明，马斯洛需求层次理论的层级划分是错误的——人们几乎同时追求所有这些需求。毫无疑问，大多数饥饿的人宁愿吃东西也不愿超越饥饿，但中间阶段的需求存在着大量的重叠。

Subsequent research suggests that the hierarchical aspect of Maslow’s theory is bogus—people pursue all of these needs pretty much simultaneously. There’s no question that most starving men would rather eat than transcend, but there’s an awful lot of overlap in the middle.

人们谈论“自身利益”时，通常会提到身体、安全、尊重这三个层面。如果说话者比较感性，有时也会提及归属感。很少有营销人员或管理者会深入探讨这些范畴之外的内容。即使是诉求……那些看似属于美学范畴的东西，实际上往往与尊重有关，只是披上了外衣（例如，豪华汽车广告）。

When people talk about “self-interest,” they’re typically invoking the Physical, Security, and Esteem layers. Sometimes Belonging gets acknowledged if the speaker is touchy-feely. Not many marketers or managers venture far beyond these categories. Even appeals that seem to fall under the Aesthetic category are often really Esteem-related, but in disguise (e.g., a luxury-auto ad).

人们之所以关注这些特定类别，或许有其充分的理由。也许这些才是真正重要的。至于其他类别——自我实现、超越等等——则显得有些学术化。近期的研究探讨了这个问题，有助于我们了解究竟是马斯洛的哪些类别更能引起人们的关注。

There could be a very good reason that people focus on those particular categories. Maybe those are the ones that truly matter. The rest of them—Self-actualization, Transcendence, and so on—do seem a bit academic. Recent research has explored this question, helping to shed light on which of Maslow’s categories made people care.

我假设一家公司承诺，如果员工达到一定的绩效目标，就给予1000美元的奖金。有三种不同的方式可以向员工发放奖金：

Imagine that a company offers its employees a $1,000 bonus if they meet certain performance targets. There are three different ways of presenting the bonus to the employees:

  1. 想想这 1000 美元意味着什么：可以作为新车的首付，或者用于你一直想做的房屋改造。

  2. Think of what that $1,000 means: a down payment on a new car or that new home improvement you’ve been wanting to make.

  3. 想想看，如果你的银行账户里有1000美元以备不时之需，安全感会大大提升。

  4. Think of the increased security of having that $1,000 in your bank account for a rainy day.

  5. 想想这1000美元意味着什么：公司认可你对公司整体业绩的重要性。公司不会白花钱。

  6. Think of what the $1,000 means: the company recognizes how important you are to its overall performance. It doesn’t spend money for nothing.

当被问及个人更倾向于哪种定位时，大多数人会选择第三种。这种定位有助于提升自尊心——至于第一种和第二种，1000美元既可以花掉也可以存起来，这不是很明显吗？我们大多数人都能毫不费力地想象自己花掉1000美元的情景。（喜欢想象自己存钱的人就比较少见了。）

When people are asked which positioning would appeal to them personally, most of them say No. 3. It’s good for the self-esteem—and, as for No. 1 and No. 2, isn’t it kind of obvious that $1,000 can be spent or saved? Most of us have no trouble at all visualizing ourselves spending $1,000. (It’s a bit less common to find people who like to visualize themselves saving.)

但这里有个转折：当被问及哪种职位对其他人（而非他们自己）来说最佳时，人们会把最能带来成就感的职位排在第一位，其次是第二位。也就是说，我们受自尊心驱动，而其他人则受首付激励驱动。这一洞察解释了……几乎所有大型组织中激励机制的结构方式都受到了影响。

Here’s the twist, though: When people are asked which is the best positioning for other people (not them), they rank No. 1 most fulfilling, followed by No. 2. That is, we are motivated by self-esteem, but others are motivated by down payments. This single insight explains almost everything about the way incentives are structured in most large organizations.

或者考虑一下同样的任务的另一种情况。假设你正试图说服某人接受一份对公司成功至关重要的部门的新工作。以下是三种可能的求职陈述：

Or consider another version of the same task. Let’s say you’re trying to persuade someone to take a new job in a department that’s crucial to the company’s success. Here are three possible pitches for the new job:

想想这份工作能提供多少保障。它非常重要，公司始终需要有人担任这个职位。

Think about how much security this job provides. It’s so important that the company will always need someone in this job.

想想这份工作带来的关注度。因为这份工作非常重要，很多人都会关注你的表现。

Think about the visibility provided by this job. Because the job is so important, a lot of people will be watching your performance.

想想看，从事这样一份核心工作会有多么有意义。它提供了一个难得的机会，让你了解公司真正的运作方式。

Think about how rewarding it will be to work in such a central job. It offers a unique opportunity to learn how the company really works.

我们与他人之间的鸿沟再次扩大。大多数人表示，第三点——诉诸学习——对他们来说最具激励作用。这些人预测，其他人则会被第一点（安全感）和第二点（尊重）所激励。

The chasm between ourselves and others opens again. Most people say No. 3—an appeal to Learning—would be most motivating for them. Those same people predict that others would be most motivated by No. 1 (Security) and No. 2 (Esteem).

换句话说，我们很多人都觉得别人都住在马斯洛需求层次理论的底层——我们可能住着顶层公寓，但其他人却住在底层。如果我们在马斯洛需求层次理论的底层停留太久，就会忽略很多激励他人的机会。这并不是说“底层”的需求——或者说，为了避免使用层次论的比喻，我们指的是那些更具体、更物质化的需求——没有激励作用。当然有。我们都喜欢拿到奖金，都渴望工作稳定，都希望融入群体。但是，如果我们只关注这些需求，就会错失挖掘更深层次激励的机会。

In other words, a lot of us think everyone else is living in Maslow’s basement—we may have a penthouse apartment, but everyone else is living below. The result of spending too much time in Maslow’s basement is that we may overlook lots of opportunities to motivate people. It’s not that the “bottom floors”—or the more tangible, physical needs, to avoid the hierarchy metaphor—aren’t motivational. Of course they are. We all like to get bonuses and to have job security and to feel like we fit in. But to focus on these needs exclusively robs us of the chance to tap more profound motivations.

运用这些更深层次的动机的一个绝佳例子是一位美国陆军退役军人——他不是战场指挥官，而是一位食堂经理。

A great example of using these more profound motivations involves a retired member of the U.S. Army—not a battlefield commander but a guy who ran a mess hall.

在伊拉克用餐

Dining in Iraq

军队的伙食不出所料：寡淡无味，煮得过熟，而且量巨大。菜肴上不会点缀欧芹。食堂简直就是卡路里工厂，为士兵提供完成任务所需的能量。一句古老的军队谚语说：“军队靠的是胃。”

Army food is just about what you’d expect: bland, overcooked, and prepared in massive quantities. The dishes are not garnished with sprigs of parsley. The mess halls are essentially calorie factories, giving the troops the fuel they need to do their jobs. An old Army proverb says, “An Army travels on its stomach.”

位于巴格达机场外的飞马餐厅（Pegasus）却有着截然不同的名声。在飞马餐厅，烤肋排烹制得恰到好处。水果拼盘琳琅满目，包括西瓜、猕猴桃和葡萄。据说，有些士兵甚至会从绿区（巴格达戒备森严的美军控制区）驱车前往飞马餐厅，沿着伊拉克最险峻的道路之一，只为吃上一顿饭。

The Pegasus chow hall, just outside the Baghdad airport, has developed a different reputation. At Pegasus, the prime rib is perfectly prepared. The fruit platter is a beautiful assortment of watermelon, kiwi fruit, and grapes. There are legends of soldiers driving to Pegasus from the Green Zone (the well-protected Americanized area of Baghdad), along one of the most treacherous roads in Iraq, just to eat a meal.

负责“飞马”餐饮项目的弗洛伊德·李，在伊拉克战争爆发时已经从海军陆战队和陆军的厨师岗位上退休，结束了他长达25年的军旅生涯。为了这份工作，他又重新出山。“上帝给了我第二次机会，让我能够继续为士兵们提供餐饮，”他说。“我一生都在等待这份工作，而现在，我来到了巴格达。”

Floyd Lee, the man in charge of Pegasus, was retired from his twenty-five-year career as a Marine Corps and Army cook when the Iraq war began. He came out of retirement to take the job. “The good Lord gave me a second chance to feed soldiers,” he said. “I’ve waited for this job all my life, and here I am in Baghdad.”

李深知当兵的艰辛。士兵们经常每天工作十八个小时，一周七天无休。在伊拉克，危险如影随形。李希望“飞马”餐厅能为士兵们提供一处远离战乱的休憩之所。他对自己的领导使命有着清晰的认识：“在我看来，我不仅负责餐饮服务，更要负责士气。”

Lee is well aware that being a soldier is relentlessly difficult. The soldiers often work eighteen-hour days, seven days a week. The threat of danger in Iraq is constant. Lee wants Pegasus to provide a respite from the turmoil. He’s clear about his leadership mission: “As I see it, I am not just in charge of food service; I am in charge of morale.”

想想看：我负责士气。按照马斯洛的需求层次理论，李追求的是超越层次。

Think about that: I am in charge of morale. In terms of Maslow’s hierarchy, Lee is going for Transcendence.

李的愿景体现在他员工每天采取的数百项细微行动中。在飞马餐厅，普通食堂的白色墙壁上挂满了运动横幅。窗户上装饰着金色饰物，桌布是带有流苏的绿色。刺眼的荧光灯被装有柔和灯泡的吊扇灯取代。服务员戴着高高的白色厨师帽。

This vision manifests itself in hundreds of small actions taken by Lee’s staff on a daily basis. At Pegasus, the white walls of the typical mess hall are covered with sports banners. There are gold treatments on the windows, and green tablecloths with tassels. The harsh fluorescent lights have been replaced by ceiling fans with soft bulbs. The servers wear tall white chef’s hats.

飞马餐厅之所以以美食闻名，最令人称奇的是，它使用的原材料与所有人使用的完全相同。其他餐厅也一样。飞马餐厅和其他餐厅一样，供应同样的21天陆军菜单，食材也来自相同的供应商。真正与众不同的是这里的态度。一位厨师会仔细挑选每日送来的水果，剔除坏葡萄，挑选西瓜和猕猴桃最好的部分，精心制作完美的水果拼盘。到了晚上，甜点桌上摆放着五种馅饼和三种蛋糕。周日的烤肋排要腌制整整两天。一位来自新奥尔良的厨师会订购香料，邮寄到伊拉克，为主菜增添风味。一位甜点师形容她的草莓蛋糕“性感而诱人”——这两个形容词以前从未用来形容过军队的食物。

The remarkable thing about Pegasus’s reputation for great food is that Pegasus works with exactly the same raw materials that everyone else does. Pegasus serves the same twenty-one-day Army menu as other dining halls. Its food comes from the same suppliers. It’s the attitude that makes the difference. A chef sorts through the daily fruit shipment, culling the bad grapes, selecting the best parts of the watermelon and kiwi, to prepare the perfect fruit tray. At night, the dessert table features five kinds of pie and three kinds of cake. The Sunday prime rib is marinated for two full days. A cook from New Orleans orders spices that are mailed to Iraq to enhance the entrées. A dessert chef describes her strawberry cake as “sexual and sensual”—two adjectives never before applied to Army food.

李意识到，端菜是一份工作，但提升士气却是一项使命。提升士气需要创造力、尝试和精湛技艺，而端菜只需要一把汤勺。

Lee realizes that serving food is a job, but improving morale is a mission. Improving morale involves creativity and experimentation and mastery. Serving food involves a ladle.

一位每周日都来佩加索斯餐厅吃晚餐的士兵说：“待在这里，你会忘记自己身处伊拉克。”李正在挖掘马斯洛需求层次理论中那些被人们遗忘的层面——审美需求、学习需求和超越需求。他重新定义了餐厅的使命，激励着同事们在沙漠中创造了一片绿洲。

One of the soldiers who commute to Pegasus for Sunday dinner said, “The time you are in here, you forget you’re in Iraq.” Lee is tapping into Maslow’s forgotten categories—the Aesthetic, Learning, and Transcendence needs. In redefining the mission of his mess hall, he has inspired his co-workers to create an oasis in the desert.

爆米花机与政治学

The Popcorn Popper and Political Science

就连邮购文案撰稿人约翰·卡普尔斯也承认，除了狭隘的自身利益之外，还有其他强大的动机。他讲了一个营销人员的故事，这位营销人员当时正在推广一部旨在帮助消防员的消防安全教育影片。他所学的理论是，消费者诉求主要有三种：性、贪婪和恐惧。

Even John Caples, the mail-order copywriter, admits that there are powerful motivations outside narrow self-interest. He tells a story about a marketer who was promoting a new educational film on fire safety that was intended to help firemen. This marketer had been taught that there are three basic consumer appeals: sex, greed, and fear.

这位营销人员的直觉是，在这种情况下，利用贪婪心理最有效。他想出了几个免费赠送的点子，希望能说服消防员们去看这部电影。他开始给各个消防队打电话，了解哪种赠送方式最受欢迎。每次打电话，他都会介绍这部新电影，然后问：“你们想不想看看这部电影，看看是否适合购买用于你们的教育项目？” 得到的回答几乎都是热情洋溢的“想！”

The marketer’s instinct was that greed would work best in this situation. He came up with a couple of ideas for free giveaways that would persuade firemen to check out the film. He began calling local units to figure out which giveaway would have the most appeal. When he called, he would describe the new film and ask, “Would you like to see the film for possible purchase for your educational programs?” The universal answer was an enthusiastic “Yes!”

第二个问题测试了他两种版本的贪婪诉求：“作为感谢消防员们观看电影的礼物，他们会更喜欢一台大型电动爆米花机，还是一套上好的厨师雕刻刀具？”

The second question tested two versions of his greed appeal: “Would your firefighters prefer a large electric popcorn popper or an excellent set of chef’s carving knives as a thank-you for reviewing the film?”

前两次通话都对这个问题给出了明确的答案：“你觉得我们会因为一个该死的爆米花机就启用消防安全程序吗？！”

The first two calls yielded definitive answers to this question: “Do you think we’d use a fire safety program because of some #*$@%! popcorn popper?!”

营销人员不再询问免费赠品的情况了。

The marketer stopped asking about the free gifts.

S哦，有时候，出于自身利益的考量会促使人们关心他人，而有时候，却会适得其反。我们该如何看待这种现象呢？如果我们从政治角度来看待这个问题，就会发现其中的奥秘。人们普遍认为，选民都是利己主义者。如果一项提案旨在提高高收入人群的边际税率，我们自然会预期富人会投反对票，而其他人则会投赞成票。

So, sometimes self-interest helps people care, and sometimes it backfires. What are we to make of this? The mystery deepens if we consider politics. The conventional wisdom is that voters are paragons of self-interest. If there’s a proposal on the table to raise the marginal tax rate on the highest incomes, we expect rich people to vote against it and everyone else to vote for it.

事实上，这种传统观点是错误的。并没有太多证据表明狭隘的自身利益能够预测公众舆论。1998年，密歇根大学政治学教授唐纳德·金德（Donald Kinder）撰写了一篇颇具影响力的综述，回顾了过去三十年来关于这一主题的研究。他将自身利益对政治观点的影响总结为“微不足道”。微不足道！金德写道：

Actually, this conventional wisdom is wrong. There’s not much evidence that public opinion can be predicted by narrow self-interest. In 1998, Donald Kinder, a professor of political science at the University of Michigan, wrote an influential survey of thirty years of research on this topic. He summarizes the effects of self-interest on political views as “trifling.” Trifling! Kinder writes:

面对平权​​行动，白人和黑人美国人在形成观点时，不会考虑个人得失。失业者不会支持旨在缓解经济困境的政策。医疗需求者并不比拥有全额医保的人更倾向于支持政府医保。公立学校学生的家长并不比其他公民更支持政府教育援助。可能被征召入伍的美国人并不比其他人更反对军事干预或正在进行的冲突升级。在外工作的女性与家庭主妇在某些方面并无差异。她们支持旨在促进女性就业的政策。在诸如为消除学校种族隔离而实行的种族校车接送、禁酒条例、大学强制性考试、住房政策、双语教育、遵守法律、对法律纠纷解决的满意度、枪支管制等诸多问题上，她们都把自身利益放在了次要位置。

When faced with affirmative action, white and black Americans come to their views without calculating personal harms or benefits. The unemployed do not line up behind policies designed to alleviate economic distress. The medically needy are no more likely to favor government health insurance than the fully insured. Parents of children in public schools are not more likely to support government aid to education than other citizens. Americans who are likely to be drafted are not more likely to oppose military intervention or escalating conflicts that are under way. Women employed outside the home do not differ from homemakers in their support of policies intended to benefit women at work. On such diverse matters as racial busing for the purpose of school desegregation, anti-drinking ordinances, mandatory college examinations, housing policy, bilingual education, compliance with laws, satisfaction with the resolution of legal disputes, gun control and more, self-interest turns out to be quite unimportant.

这些发现令人震惊地违反直觉。如果人们不维护自身利益，那么他们究竟在维护谁的利益呢？

These findings are bracingly counterintuitive. If people aren’t supporting their own self-interest, whose interests are they supporting?

答案并非一成不变。首先，当一项公共政策的影响显著、切实且立竿见影时，自身利益似乎确实至关重要。例如，1978 年加利福尼亚州的一项名为“第 13 号提案”的投票倡议，要求大幅降低房产税，但同时也大幅削减学校、图书馆、警察和消防部门等公共服务的预算。在这个问题上，厌倦了房价上涨带来的巨额税收的房主们投票支持了第 13 号提案。而图书馆员和消防员等人则投了反对票。其次，即使自身利益并不直接决定我们的立场，它也会影响我们关注的内容。例如，在第 13 号提案问题上，房主和公务员更有可能形成自己的看法——即使他们的看法与他们的个人利益相悖。

The answer is nuanced. First, self-interest does seem to matter, quite a bit, when the effects of a public policy are significant, tangible, and immediate. For example, in California in 1978, a ballot initiative called Proposition 13 called for a sharp reduction in property taxes in exchange for equally sharp reductions in public services such as schools, libraries, and police and fire departments. On this issue, homeowners—tired of the huge tax increases that accompany rising property values—voted for Proposition 13. Librarians and firefighters, among others, voted against it. Second, self-interest shapes what we pay attention to, even if it doesn’t dictate our stance. For example, on Proposition 13 homeowners and public employees were more likely to have a well-formed opinion on the initiative—even if their opinion was inconsistent with their personal self-interest.

但自利并非全部原因。平等、个人主义、政府理念、人权等等原则，即使与我们眼前的利益相悖，对我们来说也可能至关重要。我们或许不喜欢某些边缘政治团体的观点，但仍然支持他们发表言论的权利，因为我们珍视言论自由。

But self-interest isn’t the whole story. Principles—equality, individualism, ideals about government, human rights, and the like—may matter to us even when they violate our immediate self-interest. We may dislike hearing the views of some fringe political group but support its right to speak because we treasure free speech.

或许这个故事最重要的部分是：“群体利益”往往比个人利益更能预测政治观点。金德指出，人们在形成观点时，似乎问的不是“这对我有什么好处？”，而是“这对我的群体有什么好处？”。我们的群体归属可能基于种族、阶级、宗教、性别、地域等因素。政党、行业，或无数其他方面的差异。

And perhaps the most important part of the story is this: “Group interest” is often a better predictor of political opinions than self-interest. Kinder says that in forming opinions people seem to ask not “What’s in it for me?” but, rather, “What’s in it for my group?” Our group affiliation may be based on race, class, religion, gender, region, political party, industry, or countless other dimensions of difference.

斯坦福大学教授詹姆斯·马奇提出了一个相关的观点，他认为我们应该使用两种基本模型来做决策。第一种模型涉及计算后果。我们会权衡各种方案，评估每个方案的价值，然后选择能给我们带来最大价值的方案。这种模型是经济学课堂上关于决策的标准观点：人们都是自利且理性的。理性人会问自己：哪款沙发在价格范围内能给我提供最大的舒适度和最佳的美观度？哪位政治候选人最能维护我的经济和社会利益？第二种模型则截然不同。它假设人们基于自身身份做出决策。他们会问自己三个问题：我是谁？这是什么样的情况？像我这样的人在这种情况下会怎么做？

A related idea comes from James March, a professor at Stanford University, who proposes that we use two basic models to make decisions. The first model involves calculating consequences. We weigh our alternatives, assessing the value of each one, and we choose the alternative that yields us the most value. This model is the standard view of decision-making in economics classes: People are self-interested and rational. The rational agent asks, Which sofa will provide me with the greatest comfort and the best aesthetics for the price? Which political candidate will best serve my economic and social interests? The second model is quite different. It assumes that people make decisions based on identity. They ask themselves three questions: Who am I? What kind of situation is this? And what do people like me do in this kind of situation?

请注意，在第二种模型中，人们并没有分析自身的后果或结果。这里没有计算，只有规范和原则。像我这样的东南部会计师更有可能购买哪款沙发？好莱坞的佛教徒应该支持哪位政治候选人？这几乎就像人们参考了一种理想的自我形象：像我这样的人会怎么做？

Notice that in the second model people aren’t analyzing the consequences or outcomes for themselves. There are no calculations, only norms and principles. Which sofa would someone like me—a Southeastern accountant—be more likely to buy? Which political candidate should a Hollywood Buddhist get behind? It’s almost as if people consulted an ideal self-image: What would someone like me do?

第二种决策模型有助于解释消防员为何对爆米花机感到愤怒。请记住，爆米花机并非贿赂。如果营销人员说：“为你们消防站订购这部电影，我就送你们一台爆米花机给你们家人”，显然大多数人会出于道德原因拒绝这项提议。相反，这项提议并无不妥：我们赠送您一台爆米花机，以感谢您抽出时间评论这部电影。无论您对电影的评价如何，您都可以获得爆米花机。接受这项提议并无任何不道德之处。

This second model of decision-making helps shed light on why the firefighters got angry about the popcorn popper. Bear in mind that the popcorn popper wasn’t a bribe. If the marketer had said, “Order this film for your firehouse and I’ll give you a popcorn popper for your family,” clearly most people would reject the offer on ethical grounds. On the contrary, the offer was innocuous: We will give you a popcorn popper to thank you for the trouble you’re taking to review the film. You can have the popper regardless of your decision on the film. There’s nothing unethical about accepting this offer.

我们还可以更进一步：从自利的角度出发，价值从利益最大化的角度来看，拒绝这个提议简直愚蠢至极。如果你选择方案A，你就能得到一台爆米花机；如果你选择方案B，你就什么也得不到。其他条件都一样。所以，除非爆米花在你眼里会造成价值损失，否则你最好选择方案A。

And we can go further than that: From a self-interested, value maximizing point of view, it is simply stupid to turn down this offer. If you make Decision A, you end up with a popcorn popper. If you make Decision B, you end up with no popcorn popper. Everything else is the same. So unless popcorn destroys value in your world, you’d better make Decision A.

但从身份认同模型的角度来看，拒绝爆米花机完全合情合理。其思考过程可能是这样的：“我是一名消防员。你给我爆米花机是为了让我去看一部安全宣传片。但消防员可不是那种需要小礼物来激励自己学习安全知识的人。我们冒着生命危险冲进燃烧的建筑物救人。你竟然暗示我需要爆米花机，真是太可耻了！”

But from the perspective of the identity model of decision-making, turning down the popper makes perfect sense. The thought process would be more like this: “I’m a firefighter. You’re offering me a popcorn popper to get me to view a film on safety. But firefighters aren’t the kind of people who need little gifts to motivate us to learn about safety. We risk our lives, going into burning buildings to save people. Shame on you for implying that I need a popcorn popper!”

这两种决策模型并非无法融合。如果营销人员提出向学校的消防安全项目捐赠五十美元，以换取消防员观看这部电影呢？这种提议是否会侵犯消防员的身份认同感，这​​一点尚不明确。

There are ways to unite these two decision models. What if the marketer had offered to donate fifty dollars to a school’s fire-safety program in exchange for the firemen’s viewing the film? It’s less clear that this offer would have violated the firefighters’ sense of identity.

利己主义固然重要，这一点毋庸置疑。我们可以通过迎合人们的利己主义来激发他们的关注。但这会限制我们的思维方式。总是围绕利己主义构建我们的想法，就像总是只用一种颜色作画一样，既会让我们自己感到窒息，也会让别人感到乏味。

Self-interest is important. There’s no question that we can make people care by appealing to it. But it makes for a limited palette. Always structuring our ideas around self-interest is like always painting with one color. It’s stifling for us and uninspiring for others.

飞马餐厅经理弗洛伊德·李说得对。他本可以完全从自身利益出发来激励员工：比如，如果员工努力工作，就让他们每天晚上提前十分钟下班；或者让他们优先挑选牛排。但他没有这样做，而是帮助塑造了一种飞马餐厅的特色：飞马餐厅的厨师负责的是士气，而不是食物。你可以想象，帐篷里的员工们会反复思考：在这种情况下，飞马餐厅的人应该怎么做？

Floyd Lee, the manager of the Pegasus dining hall, has it right. He could have generated motivation through a strict self-interest appeal: perhaps by offering to let his employees off ten minutes early every night if they worked hard, or by giving them the first choice of the steaks. Instead, he helped create a kind of Pegasus identity: A Pegasus chef is in charge of morale, not food. You can imagine hundreds of decisions being made by staffers in the tent who think to themselves, What should a Pegasus person do in this situation?

诊所

CLINIC

对代数的需求与马斯洛需求层次理论

The Need for Algebra and Maslow’s Basement

情况： 有记载以来，每一位代数老师都不得不面对学生提出的两个问题：“我为什么要学这个？我什么时候会用到这个？”本课程将探讨三种尝试回答这些问题的方法。

THE SITUATION: Every algebra teacher in recorded history has had to deal with two student questions: “Why do I need to know this? When will I ever use this?” This Clinic examines three attempts to answer these questions.

• • •

• • •

信息 1：在 1993 年举行的“全民代数”会议上，针对“为什么要学习代数”这个问题，提出了以下几点。

MESSAGE 1: In a 1993 conference on “Algebra for All,” the following points were made in response to the question “Why study algebra?”

  • 代数提供了从特殊到一般的方法。它包括发现集合中各项之间的模式，并发展思考和与他人交流这些模式所需的语言。

  • Algebra provides methods for moving from the specific to the general. It involves discovering the patterns among items in a set and developing the language needed to think about and communicate it to others.

  • 代数提供了操作符号的方法，使我们能够理解周围的世界。

  • Algebra provides procedures for manipulating symbols to allow for understanding of the world around us.

  • 代数提供了一种通过数学模型来理解我们所处世界的方法。

  • Algebra provides a vehicle for understanding our world through mathematical models.

  • 代数是研究变量的科学。它使我们能够通过识别变量（数值会发生变化的量）以及在数据中建立或发现结构来处理大量数据。

  • Algebra is the science of variables. It enables us to deal with large bodies of data by identifying variables (quantities which change in value) and by imposing or finding structures within the data.

  • 代数是描述和推理变量之间关系的基本思想和技巧体系。

  • Algebra is the basic set of ideas and techniques for describing and reasoning about relations between variable quantities.

对第一条消息的评论：这条消息说明了知识诅咒带来的问题。想必这次会议聚集了一群代数专家，他们提出了一个在其他专家看来似乎合理的答案。但让我们面对现实吧：威尔哪个不安分的学生一听到代数“提供了一系列操作符号的方法，从而帮助我们理解世界”就立刻加入学习代数的行列呢？作为代数的定义，以上几点似乎很有道理。但作为学习代数的理由，它们却行不通。我们需要传递一个能让学生真正关心代数的信息。

COMMENTS ON MESSAGE 1: This message illustrates the problems posed by the Curse of Knowledge. Presumably, this conference was filled with a group of algebra experts and they came up with an answer that seemed plausible to other experts. But let’s get real: Will any restless student jump on the algebra bandwagon after being told that it “provides procedures for manipulating symbols to allow for understanding of the world”? As a definition of algebra, the bullets above seem quite logical. But as reasons for studying algebra, they don’t work. We need a message that makes students care about algebra.

• • •

• • •

信息 2：以下回复是我们根据网上流传的几个例子编造的：

MESSAGE 2: We made up the following response. It was inspired by several examples that we saw floating around the Internet:

我经常这样跟学生解释为什么要学习代数：

Here’s what I tell my students about why they need to learn algebra:

  • 你需要它才能获得高中毕业文凭。

  • You need it to get your high school diploma.

  • 你以后学习的每一门数学和科学课程都需要代数知识。

  • Every future math and science class you take will require a knowledge of algebra.

  • 要想考上好大学，你需要有良好的数学成绩。

  • To get admitted to a good college, you’ll need a good record in math.

  • 即使你从未打算上大学，你在代数中学到的推理能力也会帮助你买房、制定预算等等。

  • And even if you don’t ever plan to attend college, the reasoning skills you learn in algebra will help you buy a home, create a budget, etc.

我哥哥是一家高科技公司的销售代表……他以前在学校数学一直不好，但现在他意识到，他在数学课上付出的努力提高了他的分析能力，也让他能更好地向客户进行演示。

My brother is a sales rep for a high-tech firm … he always had trouble with math in school but now realizes the hard work he put into the course has improved his analytical skills and has made him a better presenter to his clients.

对第二条信息的评论：这位老师用务实的语言避免了知识的诅咒，但他仍然停留在马斯洛需求层次理论的底层。为什么要学习代数？第一个原因是：你必须学习，因为你必须学习。第二个原因是：你必须学习，这样你才能学习更多。其主要诉求是尊重——渴望获得认可和地位，渴望证明自己的能力。其中最有效的部分是……故事讲述了作者的弟弟，他后来意识到自己在数学上的苦苦挣扎最终得到了回报。这个弟弟的故事极具感染力，逐渐发展成一个近乎卡普尔斯式的胜利故事。（“当我把方程式搞砸时，他们都嘲笑我；但当我拿下客户时……”）

COMMENTS ON MESSAGE 2: This teacher avoids the Curse of Knowledge by speaking practically, but he stays close to Maslow’s Basement. Why study algebra? The first reason: You have to do it because you have to do it. The second: You have to do it so that you can do more of it. The primary appeal is to Esteem—the desire to be competent, to gain approval and status. The most effective part is the part about the author’s brother, who later realized that his struggles with math paid off. The brother story is an Esteem appeal that builds in an almost Caples-esque victory story. (“They laughed when I botched the equation, but when I won the account …”)

• • •

• • •

信息 3：这是高中代数老师迪恩·谢尔曼对高中教师之间关于此话题的网络讨论的回应：

MESSAGE 3: This is a response from a high school algebra teacher, Dean Sherman, to an Internet discussion of this topic among high school teachers:

我的九年级学生很难理解直线方程标准形式的用途，他们不禁问道：“我们什么时候会需要它呢？”

My grade 9 students have difficulty appreciating the usefulness of the Standard Form of the equation of a line, prompting them to ask, “When are we ever going to need this?”

这个问题过去一直困扰着我，因此我会想方设法为自己教的每件事寻找理由。现在我会说：“永远不会。你永远不会用到这些。”

This question used to really bother me, and I would look, as a result, for justification for everything I taught. Now I say, “Never. You will never use this.”

然后我会提醒他们，人们举重不是为了应对某天有人在街上把他们撞倒，然后把杠铃压在他们胸口的情况。举重是为了能够撞倒防守队员，或者拎着杂货，或者抱起孙子孙女，第二天不会浑身酸痛。做数学题是为了提高逻辑思维能力，这样才能成为更优秀的律师、医生、建筑师、监狱长或父母。

I then go on to remind them that people don’t lift weights so that they will be prepared should, one day, [someone] knock them over on the street and lay a barbell across their chests. You lift weights so that you can knock over a defensive lineman, or carry your groceries or lift your grandchildren without being sore the next day. You do math exercises so that you can improve your ability to think logically, so that you can be a better lawyer, doctor, architect, prison warden or parent.

数学是脑力训练。对大多数人来说，它是达到目的的手段，而不是目的本身。

MATH IS MENTAL WEIGHT TRAINING. It is a means to an end (for most people), not an end in itself.

对第三条留言的评论：这是一条很棒的回复。请注意我们在书中已经看到过的元素：出人意料的开头吸引注意力（“永远不会。你永远不会用到它”）。此外，类比的运用也十分巧妙——他利用我们已有的举重认知模式来改变我们“学习代数”的模式（也就是说，并非将来你会用到它）。你将每天都需要计算直线的斜率；这相当于锻炼你的大脑肌肉）。

COMMENTS ON MESSAGE 3: This is a great response. Note the elements we’ve seen before in the book: The surprise opening to grab attention (“Never. You will never use this”). Also, the use of analogy is brilliant—he taps our existing schema of weight lifting to change our model of “learning algebra” (i.e., it’s not that in the future you’re going to have a daily need to find the slope of a line; it’s that you’re making your brain more muscular).

他也在马斯洛的需求层次理论中不断向上攀升。这里吸引他的是更高层次的需求，例如学习和自我实现。其理念是，学习代数能让你更好地发挥自身潜力。

He is also moving up Maslow’s hierarchy. The appeal here is to higher levels like Learning and Self-actualization. The idea is that learning algebra makes you realize more of your potential.

记分卡
检查清单    消息 1    消息 2    消息 3
简单的 -    -   
意外    -    -   
具体的 -       
可信的 -    -    -
情绪 -       
故事 -        -

妙语： “数学是心理负重训练”提醒我们，即使在最平凡的情况下，也有机会走出马斯洛的需求层次理论的底层，进入更高层次的动机。

PUNCH LINE: “Math is mental weight training” reminds us that, even in the most mundane situations, there’s an opportunity to move out of Maslow’s basement and into the higher levels of motivation.

别招惹德州

Don’t Mess with Texas

丹·西雷克是美国顶尖的垃圾问题研究员。他曾与从纽约到阿拉斯加的十六个州合作开展反垃圾倡议。他通常会先随机选择一些路段——从州际公路到乡村小路——然后亲自步行勘察，双手各拿一个计数器，手动统计垃圾数量。

Dan Syrek is the nation’s leading researcher on litter. He has worked with sixteen states—from New York to Alaska—on antilitter initiatives. He often begins his projects by selecting random stretches of road—from interstates to farm roads—and walking the roads personally, a clicker in each hand, manually counting litter.

上世纪80年代，赛雷克和他位于萨克拉门托的机构——应用研究所——受聘于德克萨斯州政府。德克萨斯州曾面临严重的垃圾问题。该州每年花费2500万美元用于清理，而且费用每年还在以15%的速度增长。州政府试图通过设置“请勿乱扔垃圾”的标语、在路边放置大量标有“请帮忙清理”的垃圾桶等方式来鼓励人们养成良好的行为习惯，但收效甚微。为此，德克萨斯州聘请了Syrek公司来制定新的清理策略。

In the 1980s, Syrek and his Sacramento-based organization, the Institute for Applied Research, were hired by the state of Texas. Texas had a serious litter problem. The state was spending $25 million per year on cleanup, and the costs were rising 15 percent per year. The state’s attempts to encourage better behavior—“Please Don’t Litter” signs, lots of roadside trash cans marked “Pitch In”—weren’t working. Texas hired Syrek to help craft a new strategy.

标准的反乱扔垃圾宣传信息往往诉诸情感，但通常只关注有限的几种情感。例如，有些宣传会唤起人们的内疚和羞耻感，比如有一则广告展现了一位美洲原住民因垃圾而流泪的画面。还有一些宣传会利用人们对可爱野生动物的喜爱，例如，有一则广告的主角是一只卡通猫头鹰，它说：“爱护环境，不要污染。”

The standard antilitter message is emotional, but it tends to focus on a limited set of emotions. There are appeals to guilt and shame, as in a spot that shows a Native American shedding a tear over litter. There are also appeals to our feelings for cuddly wildlife, such as the campaign starring a cartoon owl who says, “Give a Hoot—Don’t Pollute.”

赛瑞克知道，这种宣传方式解决不了德克萨斯州的问题。在他看来，这类广告只是“对信徒布道”。德克萨斯州真正需要做的，是接触那些对路边垃圾漠不关心的人。德克萨斯州典型的乱扔垃圾者是18到35岁的男性，开着皮卡，喜欢运动和乡村音乐。他们不喜欢权威，也不会因为可爱的猫头鹰而产生情感上的共鸣。德克萨斯州交通部的一位官员说：“对这些人来说，说‘请’根本没用。”

Syrek knew that this type of messaging wouldn’t solve Texas’s problem. In his view, those kinds of ads are just “preaching to the choir.” What Texas needed to do was reach people who weren’t inclined to shed tears over roadside trash. The profile of the typical litterer in Texas was an eighteen- to thirty-five-year-old, pickup-driving male who liked sports and country music. He didn’t like authority and he wasn’t motivated by emotional associations with cuddly owls. One member of the Texas Department of Transportation said, “Saying ‘please’ to these guys falls on deaf ears.”

“我们发现乱扔垃圾的人都是些邋遢鬼，”赛雷克说。“你得跟他们解释，他们这样做是在乱扔垃圾。”赛雷克随身带着一张照片，照片上是一个看起来很壮硕的男人开着皮卡车。“这就是我们的目标客户，”他说。“我们叫他‘巴布’。”

“We found that people who throw the stuff are real slobs,” Syrek says. “You had to explain to them that what they were doing was littering.” Syrek kept with him a photo of a macho-looking man in a pickup truck. “This is our target market,” he said. “We call him Bubba.”

对这个群体来说，基于自身利益设计的反乱扔垃圾运动不太可能奏效。毕竟，这些“小混混”不乱扔垃圾到底能得到什么好处呢？正确丢弃垃圾需要付出努力，而且似乎没有明显的回报。这种情况并不适合像卡普尔斯那样，利用贪婪或性来煽动情绪。或许可以设计一种基于恐惧的策略——强调高额罚款或其他惩罚——但这些“小混混”的反权威倾向很可能会让这种方法失效（甚至适得其反）。

Designing an antilitter campaign based on self-interest wasn’t likely to work with this group. After all, what do the Bubbas really have to gain by not littering? Throwing things away properly takes effort, for which there are no obvious rewards. The situation doesn’t lend itself to a greed or sex-based appeal, à la Caples. It might be possible to design a fear-based approach—highlighting hefty fines or other punishments—but the Bubbas’ antiauthority streak would likely render it useless (or even cause it to backfire).

赛瑞克知道，改变巴布行为的最佳方法是让他相信像他这样的人不会乱扔垃圾。根据他的调查，德克萨斯州交通部批准了一项以“别惹德克萨斯”为口号的宣传活动。

Syrek knew that the best way to change Bubba’s behavior was to convince him that people like him did not litter. Based on his research, the Texas Department of Transportation approved a campaign built around the slogan “Don’t Mess with Texas.”

最早的电视广告之一以两位在德克萨斯州颇有名气的达拉斯牛仔队球员为主角：防守端锋埃德·“高个子”·琼斯和防守截锋兰迪·怀特。广告中，他们正在高速公路边捡垃圾：

One of the earliest TV commercials featured two Dallas Cowboy players who were famous in Texas: defensive end Ed “Too-Tall” Jones and defensive tackle Randy White. In the spot, they’re picking up trash on the side of a highway:

“高个子琼斯”走向镜头说：“你看到把这东西扔出窗外的那个人了吗……你告诉他，我给他带了个话。”

Too-Tall Jones steps toward the camera and says, “You see the guy who threw this out the window … you tell him I got a message for him.”

兰迪·怀特拿着一罐啤酒走上前来说：“我也有话要对他说……”

Randy White steps forward with a beer can and says, “I got a message for him too …”

镜头外传来一个声音问道：“那是什么？”

An off-camera voice asks, “What’s that?”

怀特用拳头捏碎了罐子，威胁道：“嗯，我得亲自去见他才能把东西送过去。”

White crushes the can with his fist and says threateningly, “Well, I kinda need to see him to deliver it.”

“高个子琼斯”补充道：“别惹德克萨斯。”

Too-Tall Jones adds, “Don’t mess with Texas.”

这条广告与可爱的猫头鹰和哭泣的美洲原住民形象截然不同。

This commercial is a far cry from cute owls and weepy Native Americans.

另一则广告的主角是休斯顿太空人队的投手迈克·斯科特，他以指叉快速球闻名。斯科特说，乱扔垃圾是“德州人的行事方式”。他现场演示了他的“指叉垃圾球”，把一些垃圾扔进路边的垃圾桶，垃圾桶瞬间爆炸，燃起一道火柱。真是妙趣横生。

Another ad features Houston Astros pitcher Mike Scott, famous for his split-fingered fastball. Scott says that throwing stuff away is “the Texas thing to do.” He demonstrates his “split-fingered trashball,” hurling some litter into a roadside can, which explodes with a pillar of fire. Subtle stuff.

这场宣传活动以运动员和音乐家为主角，他们中的大多数人在德克萨斯州以外可能并不家喻户晓，但对于德州人来说，他们都是地道的德州人：休斯顿油人队四分卫沃伦·穆恩、拳击手乔治·福尔曼、蓝调吉他手史蒂夫·雷·沃恩和乡村音乐歌手杰里·杰夫·沃克。威利·纳尔逊也参与了一支广告的拍摄，广告语是：“妈妈们，告诉你们的孩子们，‘别惹德州。’”

The campaign featured athletes and musicians, most of whom probably weren’t household names outside Texas but were all well-known to Texans as Texans: Houston Oiler quarterback Warren Moon, boxer George Foreman, blues guitarist Stevie Ray Vaughan, and country artist Jerry Jeff Walker. Willie Nelson contributed an ad with the line “Mamas, tell all your babies, ‘Don’t mess with Texas.’”

但这不就是普通的明星代言吗？不，它远不止于此。比这更微妙。当然，这些广告并非完全依靠明星效应——芭芭拉·史翠珊配上“巴布”这个角色恐怕难以产生太大影响。即使是那些充满男子气概的明星，效果也未必相同。施瓦辛格虽然很有男子气概，但他身上丝毫没有体现出德州人的特质。

But isn’t this just a garden-variety celebrity endorsement? No, it’s more subtle than that. Certainly, the spots are not driven by pure celebrity—Barbra Streisand wouldn’t pack much of a punch with Bubba. And even macho celebrities wouldn’t have worked the same way. Schwarzenegger is macho but does nothing to evoke Texanness.

如果活动沿用同样的明星，但采用更传统的公益广告形式呢？比如：“我是职业拳击手乔治·福尔曼。乱扔垃圾很没品。” 这种方法也不太可能奏效：福尔曼会扮演巴布最讨厌的权威角色。

What if the campaign used the same celebrities but adopted a more conventional PSA-type approach? “I’m pro boxer George Foreman. It’s uncool to litter.” That, too, would be unlikely to work: Foreman would be stepping into the authority role that Bubba hates.

这场运动的主题是“德州人不乱扔垃圾”。值得注意的是，这些名人之所以有价值，仅仅是因为他们能够迅速塑造“德州”的形象——更确切地说，是“理想的、充满男子气概的德州人”的形象。即使是不喜欢威利·纳尔逊音乐的人，也能欣赏他身上那种德州人的特质。

The message of the campaign was Texans don’t litter. Notice that the celebrities are valuable only insofar as they can quickly establish the schema of “Texas”—or, more specifically, of “ideal, masculine Texans.” Even people who dislike Willie Nelson’s music can appreciate his quality of Texan-ness.

这项活动一经推出便大获成功。短短几个月内，接受调查的德州居民中，高达73%的人能够回忆起宣传信息，并意识到这是一条反乱扔垃圾的信息。一年之内，垃圾数量下降了29%。

The campaign was an instant success. Within a few months of the launch, an astonishing 73 percent of Texans polled could recall the message and identify it as an antilitter message. Within one year, litter had declined 29 percent.

交通部最初计划在“别惹德州”宣传活动的同时，额外拨款100万美元，加强对乱扔垃圾法律的执法力度。这是一种恐吓策略：如果你乱扔垃圾，就更有可能被抓到并受到起诉。但“别惹德州”宣传活动的效果如此强劲且立竿见影，以至于执法计划被取消了。通过向“巴布”（Bubba，德州吉祥物）传递关于身份认同的有力信息，该宣传活动使得诉诸恐惧变得不再必要。

The Department of Transportation originally planned to accompany the “Don’t Mess with Texas” campaign with a separate $1 million program to enforce litter laws more vigorously. This was a fear tactic: If you litter, you’re more likely to get caught and prosecuted. But the effect of “Don’t Mess with Texas” was so strong and immediate that the enforcement program was abandoned. By offering Bubba a compelling message about identity, the campaign made appeals to fear unnecessary.

在活动的前五年，德克萨斯州路边可见的垃圾减少了72%，道路沿线的易拉罐数量减少了81%。1988年，赛雷克发现，与其他开展类似时期反乱扔垃圾项目的州相比，德克萨斯州道路沿线的垃圾量不到其一半。

During the first five years of the campaign, visible roadside litter in Texas decreased 72 percent and the number of cans along Texas roads dropped 81 percent. In 1988, Syrek found that Texas had less than half the trash he found along the roads of other states that had run antilitter programs for comparable periods.

“别惹德州”这句口号很棒。但我们不应该把口号和背后的理念混淆。背后的理念是，Syrek可以通过向巴布展示真正的德州人不会乱扔垃圾，来让他重视垃圾问题。其理念是，巴布更容易被身份认同所打动，而不是理性的利己主义诉求。即便聘请的是一位二流文案，即便口号是“别不尊重德州”，这场运动仍然能够减少德州高速公路上的易拉罐数量。

“Don’t Mess with Texas,” as a phrase, is a great slogan. But we shouldn’t confuse the slogan with the idea. The idea was that Syrek could make Bubba care about litter by showing him that real Texans didn’t litter. The idea was that Bubba would respond to an identity appeal better than he would to a rational self-interest appeal. Even if a second-rate copywriter had been hired, and the slogan had been “Don’t Disrespect Texas,” the campaign would still have decreased cans on Texas highways.

双钢琴音乐

The Music of Duo Piano

到目前为止，我们已经探讨了三种引起人们关注的策略：利用联想（或避免联想，视情况而定）、诉诸自身利益以及诉诸身份认同。这三种策略都可能有效，但我们必须警惕老对手——知识诅咒，它会阻碍我们有效运用这些策略。

So far we’ve looked at three strategies for making people care: using associations (or avoiding associations, as the case may be), appealing to self-interest, and appealing to identity. All three strategies can be effective, but we’ve got to watch out for our old nemesis, the Curse of Knowledge, which can interfere with our ability to implement them.

2002年，奇普协助一群教授在佛罗里达州迈阿密和劳德代尔堡为非营利艺术机构的领导者举办了一场研讨会。其中一项练习旨在帮助领导者阐明并完善其机构的核心使命。向与会者提出的问题颇具挑战性：你们的机构存在的意义是什么？其他机构能否做你们所做的事情——如果可以，你们的独特之处又是什么？

In 2002, Chip helped a group of professors lead a seminar for nonprofit arts leaders in Miami and Ft. Lauderdale, Florida. One of the exercises was intended to help the leaders articulate and refine the core mission of their organization. The questions put to the attendees were difficult ones: Why does your organization exist? Can other organizations do what you do—and if so, what is it you do that is unique?

其中一个问题要求参与者定义其组织的宗旨，以激励其他人关心该组织。志愿者必须足够关心才能贡献时间，捐赠者必须足够关心才能捐献金钱，员工必须足够关心才能忠于组织（即使他们收到其他营利性组织的高薪工作邀请）。参加研讨会的组织之一是默里·德拉诺夫双钢琴基金会。轮到他们发言时，奇普请代表们朗读他们那份充满感染力的宗旨声明：

One question asked participants to define the purpose of their organization in a way that would motivate other people to care about it. Volunteers must care enough to contribute their time, donors must care enough to donate their money, and employees must care enough to stick by the organization (even when they get lucrative job offers from other, for-profit organizations). One of the organizations attending the seminar was the Murray Dranoff Duo Piano Foundation. When it was their turn, Chip asked the representatives to read their emotion-evoking purpose statement:

双钢琴团体：我们的宗旨是保护、保存和推广双钢琴音乐。

DUO PIANO GROUP: We exist to protect, preserve, and promote the music of duo piano.

C HIP：为什么保护双钢琴音乐很重要？

CHIP: Why is it important to protect the music of duo piano?

双钢琴组合：嗯，现在很少有人演奏双钢琴音乐了。我们想让它不至于消亡。

DUO PIANO GROUP: Well, not much duo piano music is being performed anymore. We want to keep it from dying out.

一位与会者后来承认，他第一次听到“双钢琴”这个词时，立刻想到了旅游酒吧里常见的“双钢琴对决”，以及人们醉醺醺地跟着《钢琴师》哼唱的场景。在场的一些人认为，或许我们不应该阻止双钢琴音乐的消亡，而应该加速它的消亡。

One attendee admitted later that when he first heard the phrase “duo piano” he immediately thought of the “dueling pianos” that you find in touristy bars, with people drunkenly singing along to “Piano Man.” Some people in the room thought that perhaps the death of duo piano music should not be prevented but hastened.

谈话兜了好几分钟圈子，但始终没能引起在场众人对双钢琴艺术形式的关注。最后，另一位参与者插话道：“我不想冒犯你，但如果双钢琴音乐彻底消失，世界又怎会因此而变得贫瘠呢？”

The conversation went around in circles for a few minutes without much progress in making the people in the room care about duo piano as an art form. Finally, one of the other participants chimed in: I don’t want to be rude, but why would the world be a less rich place if duo piano music disappeared completely?

双钢琴组合：（显然很惊讶）。哇……

DUO PIANO GROUP: (Clearly taken aback). Wow …

钢琴是一种绝妙的乐器。它的诞生是为了让一位演奏者就能掌控整个管弦乐队的音域和音色。没有任何其他乐器能拥有如此广阔的音域和丰富的表现力。

The piano is this magnificent instrument. It was created to put the entire range and tonal quality of the whole orchestra under the control of one performer. There is no other instrument that has the same breadth and range.

当你把两件如此精妙的乐器放在同一个房间里，演奏者可以互相呼应、互相启发，这就像是拥有了管弦乐队的音响效果，却又兼具了室内乐的亲密感。

And when you put two of these magnificent instruments in the same room, and the performers can respond to each other and build on each other, it’s like having the sound of the orchestra but the intimacy of chamber music.

这时，房间里的人都惊讶地挑起了眉毛，随即响起一阵低低的赞许声。这句话——“既有管弦乐的恢弘气势，又兼具室内乐的亲密氛围”——意味深长，令人回味无穷。房间里的人们突然明白了，为什么默里·德拉诺夫团队如此执着于双钢琴演奏，并且应该如此。

At that point, surprise brows went up around the room and there was an audible murmur of approval. This phrase—“the sound of the orchestra but the intimacy of chamber music”—was profound and evocative. Suddenly the people in the room understood, for the first time, why the Murray Dranoff team was, and should be, committed to the duo piano.

为什么默里·德拉诺夫小组花了十分钟时间？想出一个能让其他人关注的信息？你可能会认为，一个致力于双钢琴演奏的团体，应该是世界上最能解释这种音乐价值的人。

Why did it take ten minutes for the Murray Dranoff group to come up with a message that made other people care? You’d think that a group devoted to the duo piano would be in the best position of anyone on earth to explain the value of the music.
