“Do you know”: Message 2 of the Oral Rehydration Therapy Clinic is by James Grant, who, during his time at UNICEF, made changes that have been credited with saving the lives of more than 25 million children. Vaccination rates increased, for example, from 20 percent to 80 percent. This remarkable story is told in David Bornstein, How to Change the World: Social Entrepreneurs and the Power of New Ideas (Oxford: Oxford University Press, 2004). See for the quote.

“马鞍峰山姆”里克·沃伦，《标竿人生的教会》（密歇根州大急流城：宗德万出版社，1995 年）：169。沃伦的这本书解释了美国规模最大、发展最快的教会之一的组织原则。

“Saddleback Sam” Rick Warren, The Purpose-Driven Church (Grand Rapids, Mich.: Zondervan, 1995): 169. Warren’s book explains the organizing principles of one of the largest and fastest-growing churches in the country.

4. 可信的

4. Credible

“根本没有那种风度”：丹尼尔·Q·哈尼，“溃疡是由细菌引起的消息缓慢地传到医生那里”，《布法罗新闻报》 ，1996 年 2 月 11 日。

“simply didn’t have the demeanor”: Daniel Q. Haney, “News That Ulcers Are Caused by Bacteria Travels Slowly to MDs,” Buffalo News, February 11, 1996.

“尝起来像沼泽水”： Manveet Kaur，“发现‘溃疡菌’的医生”，《新海峡时报》 ，2002 年 8 月 13 日，第 6 页。

“It tasted like swamp water”: Manveet Kaur, “Doctor Who Discovered ‘Ulcer Bugs,’” New Straits Times, August 13, 2002, 6.

现代医学的一个重要主题：劳拉·贝尔，“重新审视旧病：研究发现一些慢性病可能具有传染性”，《记录报》（新泽西州北部），1997 年 3 月 24 日。

an important theme in modern medicine: Laura Beil, “A New Look at Old Ills: Research Finds Some Chronic Diseases May Be Infectious,” The Record (Northern New Jersey), March 24, 1997.

但如果我们对以下观点持怀疑态度：那些自然而然深入人心的想法是理解说服过程的重要线索，心理学领域研究说服的学者可以从中获益。传统的心理学说服研究通过构建大量论证，让人们评估其可信度，然后使用那些被评为高可信度或低可信度的论证，从而回避了可信度问题。除了少数关于权威影响的研究之外，研究人员一直避免尝试理解是什么使信息可信。然而，谣言和都市传说经常会发展出一些特征，使一些离奇的说法也变得可信。例如，我们在本章讨论的“可验证的资质”这一概念，就存在于数十个都市传说中，但却鲜少被说服研究文献提及。

But if we’re skeptical about: Naturally sticky ideas are a great source of insight about the process of persuasion, and researchers who study persuasion in psychology would benefit from studying them. Traditional studies of persuasion in psychology have sidestepped the issue of credibility by creating a bunch of arguments, having people rate them for credibility, then using the ones that are rated as having high or low credibility. With the exception of a number of studies on the impact of authority, researchers have avoided trying to understand what makes messages credible. Yet rumors and urban legends regularly evolve features that ascribe credibility to bizarre claims. The “testable credentials” idea that we discuss in the chapter, for example, has been a feature of dozens of urban legends, yet it has not been discussed by the research literature on persuasion.

133大约在 1999 年，一封电子邮件消息：关于香蕉会吃人的传说在www.snopes.com/medical/disease/bananas.asp上讨论​​。

133 Around 1999, an e-mail message: The flesh-eating banana legend is discussed at www.snopes.com/medical/disease/bananas.asp.

反权威的帕姆·拉芬：帕姆·拉芬的故事在贝拉·英格利希的文章《分享一段化为乌有的人生》（载于1998年9月20日《波士顿环球报》）中有所描述。

Pam Laffin, the Antiauthority: The story of Pam Laffin is described in Bella English, “Sharing a Life Gone Up in Smoke,” Boston Globe, September 20, 1998.

“我开始吸烟是为了让自己看起来更成熟”：摘自美国疾病控制与预防中心网站www.cdc.gov/tobacco/christy/myth1.htm。

“I started smoking to look older”: From the website of the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, www.cdc.gov/tobacco/christy/myth1.htm.

Doe基金会派了一名司机：我们感谢Spencer Robertson提供的这个例子。

The Doe Fund sent a driver: We thank Spencer Robertson for this example.

“大赚一笔”：简·哈罗德·布伦万德，《消失的搭车客： 美国都市传说及其意义》（纽约：W.W.诺顿公司，1981年），第7页。这本书在很大程度上促成了美国都市传说的热潮。多年来，民俗学家一直在撰写关于现代人民间传说的文章，但布伦万德的这本书通俗易懂，让每个人都开始听说都市传说——他们震惊地发现，全国各地都在讲述着与他们当地故事不同的版本。

“acquire a good deal”: Jan Harold Brunvand, The Vanishing Hitchhiker: American Urban Legends and Their Meanings (New York: W. W. Norton & Company, 1981), 7. This book is largely responsible for creating the urban legends craze in the United States. For years, folklorists had been writing articles about the folklore of modern people, but this book by Brunvand was accessible enough that everyone started hearing about urban legends—and they were shocked to hear that different versions of their local stories were being told by everyone else in the nation.

通过具体化论点：心理学文献中一直存在关于生动细节对记忆和可​​信度影响的争论。我们认为，现有证据令人困惑，因为研究人员没有仔细区分哪些细节支持核心信息，哪些细节会分散注意力。人们不可避免地会关注并记住生动的细节。当生动的细节支持核心信息时，信息更容易被记住，也更具说服力；但无关的生动细节也会分散人们对核心信息的注意力，降低信息的记忆度和说服力（因此，教育心理学中才会出现“诱人的细节”这一问题）。关于这些问题的精彩总结，可参阅 Ernest T. Goetz 和 Mark Sadoski 的文章“评论：诱惑的危险：分散注意力的细节还是难以理解的抽象概念？”，该文发表于《阅读研究季刊》 30 (1995)，第 500-511 页。

By making a claim tangible: There is a running debate in the psychology literature on the impact that vivid details have on memory and credibility. In our view, the evidence is confusing because researchers have not been careful about distinguishing details that support or distract from a core message. People inevitably focus on and remember vivid details. When the vivid details support the core message, it is more memorable and convincing, but irrelevant vivid details can also distract people from the core and make a message less memorable and convincing (thus the concern, in educational psychology, about “seductive details”). A good summary of the issues can be found in Ernest T. Goetz and Mark Sadoski, “Commentary: The Perils of Seduction: Distracting Details or Incomprehensible Abstractions?” Reading Research Quarterly 30 (1995), 500–11.

1986 年，Jonathan Shedler 和 Melvin Manis： Jonathan Shedler 和 Melvin Manis，“可得性启发式能否解释生动性效应？” 《人格与社会心理学杂志》 51 (1986)，26-36。

In 1986, Jonathan Shedler and Melvin Manis: Jonathan Shedler and Melvin Manis, “Can the Availability Heuristic Explain Vividness Effects?” Journal of Personality and Social Psychology 51 (1986), 26–36.

“比如说，一支足球队”：科维的例子摘自他的一本书，该书于2004 年 11 月 29 日在《财富》杂志上刊登，第 162 页。

“If, say, a soccer team”: The Covey example is from an excerpt from his book reprinted in Fortune, November 29, 2004, 162.

鲨鱼与鹿：感谢 Tim O'Hara 为鲨鱼袭击恐慌诊所第 2 条信息中的比较提供了想法。

A SHARK A DEER: We thank Tim O’Hara for the idea for the comparison in Message 2 of the Shark Attack Hysteria Clinic.

可食用织物：威廉·麦克唐纳，2003年康拉丁·冯·古格尔伯格环境纪念讲座，斯坦福大学，2003年2月11日；www.gsb.stanford.edu/news/headlines/2003_vongugelberg.shtml。另见 Andrew Curry，“绿色机器”，《美国新闻与世界报道》，2002 年 8 月 5 日，第 36 页。

Edible Fabrics: William McDonough, 2003 Conradin Von Gugelberg Memorial Lecture on the Environment, Stanford University, February 11, 2003; www.gsb.stanford.edu/news/headlines/2003_vongugelberg.shtml. See also Andrew Curry, “Green Machine,” U.S. News & World Report, August 5, 2002, 36.

“情绪储备池”： “情绪储备池”一词出自吉姆·汤普森的著作《双目标教练：尊重比赛、培养体育与人生赢家的积极教练工具》（纽约：哈珀柯林斯出版社，2003年）。书中对这项练习进行了详细描述。对于所有儿童体育教练来说，这本书都是必读之作。

“The Emotional Tank”: “Emotional Tank” is from Jim Thompson, The Double-Goal Coach: Positive Coaching Tools for Honoring the Game and Developing Winners in Sports and Life (New York: HarperCollins, 2003). The exercise is described. This book is a must-read for anyone who coaches kids’ sports.

但在美国：我们的直觉有缺陷诊所中关于各种死因的统计数据来自 2001 年美国统计摘要。

But in the United States: The statistics in the Our Intuition Is Flawed Clinic about various causes of death are from the 2001 Statistical Abstract of the United States.

NBA新秀训练营开始前几周：米歇尔·考夫曼在《迈阿密先驱报》 2003年10月5日发表的一篇精彩文章“为球员争取机会”中描述了NBA新秀训练营的情况。

A few weeks before the NBA: The NBA rookie orientation is described in a great article by Michelle Kaufman, “Making a Play for Players,” Miami Herald, October 5, 2003.

在 NFL 的新秀介绍会上：参见 Grant Wahl 和 L. Jon Wertheim，“Paternity Ward”，《体育画报》，1998 年 5 月 4 日，第 62 页。

At the NFL’s orientation: See Grant Wahl and L. Jon Wertheim, “Paternity Ward,” Sports Illustrated, May 4, 1998, 62.

5. 情绪

5. Emotional

2004 年，卡内基梅隆大学的一些研究人员： Deborah A. Small、George Loewenstein 和 Paul Slovic，“洞察力会滋生冷酷无情吗？了解可识别受害者效应对同情心的影响”，工作论文，宾夕法尼亚大学，2005 年。

In 2004, some researchers at Carnegie Mellon: Deborah A. Small, George Loewenstein, and Paul Slovic, “Can Insight Breed Callousness? The Impact of Learning About the Identifiable Victim Effect on Sympathy,” working paper, University of Pennsylvania, 2005.

本章探讨情感因素：本章重点关注情感的力量如何使人们产生共鸣，但研究表明，情感化的想法也更容易被记住。情感会增强人们对事件“要点或核心”的记忆。记忆研究者谈到“武器聚焦”——被抢劫或目睹犯罪的人往往能清晰地记住作案者的枪支或刀具，但对其他事物却记忆模糊（见下文Reisberg和Heuer的文章）。人们会记住事件的核心情感主题以及在空间或因果结构上密切相关的其他事物。因此，强调某个想法的情感内容可能是引导人们关注核心信息的一种方法。参见Daniel Reisberg和Friderike Heuer合著的《情感事件的记忆》，载于Daniel Reisberg和Paula Hertel主编的《记忆与情感》 （牛津：牛津大学出版社，2004年）。

This chapter tackles the emotional component: This chapter focuses on the power of emotions to make people care, but research suggests that emotional ideas are also more memorable. Emotions increase memory for an event’s “gist or center.” Memory researchers talk about “weapon focus”—people who have been robbed or who have witnessed crimes often remember the perpetrator’s gun or knife with great clarity but remember little else (Reisberg and Heuer, below). People remember the central emotional theme of an event and other things that are closely related in space or causal structure. Thus, highlighting the emotional content of an idea may be one way to focus people on a core message. See Daniel Reisberg and Friderike Heuer, “Memory for Emotional Events” in Memory and Emotion, ed. Daniel Reisberg and Paula Hertel (Oxford: Oxford University Press, 2004).

马克·萨多斯基及其同事发现，文本的情感方面被认为更重要（Sadoski、Goetz 和 Kangiser，1988），并且更容易被回忆起来（Sadoski 和 Quest，1990）。有趣的是，后一篇文章是众多研究发现事物更具情感因素的研究之一。当它们易于形象化时，这种做法不仅有助于理解，还能激发情感，使人们产生共鸣。Mark Sadoski 和 Z. Quest，“新闻文本的读者回忆和长期回忆：意象、情感和重要性的作用”，《阅读研究季刊》 25 (1990)，256–72。Mark Sadoski、Ernest T. Goetz 和 Suzanne Kangiser，“故事反应中的想象力：意象、情感和结构重要性之间的关系”，《阅读研究季刊》 23 (1988)，320–36。

Mark Sadoski and colleagues have found that emotional aspects of texts are rated as more important (Sadoski, Goetz, and Kangiser, 1988) and are recalled much better (Sadoski and Quest, 1990). Interestingly, the latter article is among several research studies that have found that things are more emotional when they are easy to visualize. Making things concrete not only helps make them understandable, it makes them emotional and helps people care. Mark Sadoski and Z. Quest, “Reader Recall and Long-term Recall for Journalistic Text: The Roles of Imagery, Affect, and Importance,” Reading Research Quarterly 25 (1990), 256–72. Mark Sadoski, Ernest T. Goetz, and Suzanne Kangiser, “Imagination in Story Response: Relationships Between Imagery, Affect, and Structural Importance,” Reading Research Quarterly 23 (1988), 320–36.

“我们觉得[真相广告]”： “烟雾信号”，《洛杉矶周刊》，2000 年 11 月 24 日至 30 日（也可在www.laweekly.com/ink/01/01/offbeat.php上找到）。

“We felt that [the Truth ads]”: “Smoke Signals,” LA Weekly, November 24–30, 2000 (also found at www.laweekly.com/ink/01/01/offbeat.php).

《美国公共卫生杂志》： Matthew C. Farrelly 等人撰写的《探寻真相：评估国家烟草反营销活动》一文对“真相”和“思考，不要吸烟”运动进行了比较，该文发表于《美国公共卫生杂志》 92 (2002)，第 901-7 页。

American Journal of Public Health: The comparison of the “Truth” and “Think. Don’t Smoke” campaigns is in Matthew C. Farrelly, et al, “Getting to the Truth: Evaluating National Tobacco Countermarketing Campaigns,” American Journal of Public Health 92 (2002), 901–7.

将自身与情感联系起来：这一原理自伊万·巴甫洛夫因教会狗听到铃声就流口水而获得诺贝尔奖以来就广为人知。罗伯特·西奥迪尼的著作《影响力：说服心理学》 （纽约：Quill出版社，1993年）中“喜欢”一章对联想的力量进行了有趣的探讨。西奥迪尼以一位在多雨城市工作的气象播报员的困境开篇，这位播报员经常收到仇恨邮件，因为观众会将他与他播报的新闻联系起来；他还讨论了“午餐技巧”的研究，该研究表明人们更容易支持他们在午餐时首次听到的政治言论。西奥迪尼的著作是影响力研究的经典之作，也是社会科学领域最杰出的著作之一。

associating themselves with emotions: This principle has been well-known since Ivan Pavlov won the Nobel Prize for teaching dogs to salivate in response to a bell. A fun discussion of the power of association is found in the chapter on “Liking” in Robert Cialdini’s book Influence: The Psychology of Persuasion (New York: Quill, 1993). Cialdini opens with the dilemma of the weatherman in a rainy city who regularly receives hate mail because viewers associate him with the news he delivers; he also discusses research on the “luncheon technique” that showed people were more likely to endorse political statements that they first heard while eating lunch. Cialdini’s book is the classic study on influence and one of the best books in the social sciences.

“《罗生门》 可以被视为”： C. Vognar，“日本电影传奇人物黑泽明逝世，享年 88 岁”，《达拉斯晨报》 ，1998 年 9 月 7 日，1A。

“Rashomon can be seen as”: C. Vognar, “Japanese Film Legend Kurosawa Dies at 88,” Dallas Morning News, September 7, 1998, 1A.

1929年，爱因斯坦提出抗议：爱因斯坦关于人们使用“相对论”一词方式的评论出自大卫·博达尼斯所著的《E=mc² ：世界上最著名方程式的传记》（纽约：沃克出版社，2000年）。引文如下。

In 1929, Einstein protested: Einstein’s comments about the way people used the term relativity is from David Bodanis, E = mc2: A Biography of the World’s Most Famous Equation (New York: Walker & Company, 2000). Quotes are.

斯坦福大学和耶鲁大学开展的一项研究： Chip Heath 和 Roger Gould 的论文《思想市场中的语义延伸》（斯坦福大学，2005 年）。在这篇论文中，Chip 和 Roger 还指出， “好”的极端同义词（例如，fantastic或amazing）的使用增长速度快于不那么极端的同义词（例如，okay或pretty good），而“坏”的极端同义词（例如，awful与bad）也呈现出同样的模式。要么语义延伸正在发生，要么世界正在同时变得更好和更坏。

Research conducted at Stanford and Yale: Chip Heath and Roger Gould, “Semantic Stretch in the Marketplace of Ideas,” working paper, Stanford University, 2005. In this paper, Chip and Roger also showed that extreme synonyms for the word good (e.g., fantastic or amazing) are increasing in use faster than synonyms that are less extreme (okay or pretty good), and that extreme synonyms for bad (awful versus bad) show the same pattern. Either semantic stretch is happening or the world is becoming simultaneously much better and much worse.

175体育精神曾经是一个强大的理念：吉姆·汤普森，《双重目标教练：尊重比赛、培养体育和人生赢家的积极教练工具》（纽约：哈珀柯林斯出版社，2003年）。第四章讨论了体育精神和“尊重比赛”理念存在的问题。

175 Sportsmanship was once a powerful idea: Jim Thompson, The Double-Goal Coach: Positive Coaching Tools for Honoring the Game and Developing Winners in Sports and Life (New York: HarperCollins, 2003). Chapter 4 talks about the problems with sportsmanship and the idea of Honoring the Game.

1925年，约翰·卡普尔斯（John Caples）出版了《经过检验的广告方法》（Tested Advertising Methods），这是关于邮购广告的经典著作，第五版，由弗雷德·E·哈恩（Fred E. Hahn）修订（新泽西州帕拉姆斯：普伦蒂斯霍尔出版社，1997年）。邮购广告通常显得粗糙俗气，但正如我们在书中提到的，它们是广告商能够立即获得可衡量反馈的少数途径之一，从而了解哪些广告有效，哪些无效。这意味着，了解它们为何呈现出现在的样子，往往蕴含着许多智慧——因为有人已经测试过每一个方面。

In 1925, John Caples: The classic book on mail-order advertising is John Caples, Tested Advertising Methods, 5th ed., revised by Fred E. Hahn (Paramus, N.J.: Prentice Hall, 1997). Mail-order ads are frequently schlocky, but, as we say in the text, they’re one of the few places where advertisers get immediate, measurable feedback about what is and isn’t working. That means that there’s often a lot of wisdom to be gained in understanding why they look the way they do—someone has tested every attribute.

前电视制片人杰瑞·韦斯曼 (Jerry Weissman ) 著： 《制胜之道： 讲述你的故事的艺术》 (纽约：金融时报普伦蒂斯霍尔出版社，2003 年)。引文如下：

Jerry Weissman, a former TV producer: Jerry Weissman, Presenting to Win: The Art of Telling Your Story (New York: Financial Times Prentice Hall, 2003). The quote is.

“不要说‘人们会喜欢’”: Caples/Hahn，《测试广告》，133。

“Don’t say, ‘People will enjoy’”: Caples/Hahn, Tested Advertising, 133.

坦佩的有线电视： W. Larry Gregory、Robert B. Cialdini 和 Kathleen M. Carpenter，“与自我相关的场景作为可能性估计和服从的中介：想象真的能做到吗？” 《人格与社会心理学杂志》 43 (1982): 89–99。

Cable TV in Tempe: W. Larry Gregory, Robert B. Cialdini, and Kathleen M. Carpenter, “Self-Relevant Scenarios as Mediators of Likelihood Estimates and Compliance: Does Imagining Make It So?” Journal of Personality and Social Psychology 43 (1982): 89–99.

1954 年，一位名叫亚伯拉罕·马斯洛的心理学家出版了《亚伯拉罕·马斯洛：动机与人格》（纽约：哈珀出版社，1954 年）。

In 1954, a psychologist named Abraham Maslow: Abraham Maslow, Motivation and Personality (New York: Harper, 1954).

后续研究表明，层级结构：随便翻开一本心理学入门书籍，每位教科书作者都会印上马斯洛需求层次理论的图片，因为它的图示非常精美，然后又会承认他理论中的层级结构部分并不完全适用。

Subsequent research suggests that the hierarchical: See any introductory book in psychology. Every textbook author prints a picture of Maslow’s hierarchy because it’s a great graphic, then confesses that the hierarchical aspect of his theory didn’t quite work.

想象一下，一家公司提供：奖金和新的工作框架研究来自 Chip Heath，“论代理关系的社会心理学：动机的外在理论过分强调外在奖励”，《组织行为与人类决策过程》 78 (1999): 25–62。

Imagine that a company offers: The bonus and new job-framing studies are from Chip Heath, “On the Social Psychology of Agency Relationships: Lay Theories of Motivation Overemphasize Extrinsic Rewards,” Organizational Behavior and Human Decision Processes 78 (1999): 25–62.

在伊拉克用餐：弗洛伊德·李的故事出自朱利安·E·巴恩斯的一篇精彩文章《美食绿洲》，发表于《美国新闻与世界报道》 2004 年 12 月 6 日，第 28 页。

Dining in Iraq: The Floyd Lee story is from a marvelous article by Julian E. Barnes, “A Culinary Oasis,” U.S. News & World Report, December 6, 2004, 28.

爆米花机与政治学：爆米花机的故事出自 Caples/Hahn 的《测试广告》，第 71 页。

The Popcorn Popper and Political Science: The popcorn popper story is from Caples/Hahn, Tested Advertising, 71.

面对平权​​行动：唐纳德·金德，《政治领域中的观点与行动》，载于《社会心理学手册》，丹尼尔·T·吉尔伯特、苏珊·T·菲斯克和加德纳·林德齐编，第4版（伦敦：牛津大学出版社，1988年），第778-867页。以上引文出自该书。

When faced with affirmative action: Donald Kinder, “Opinion and Action in the Realm of Politics,” in Handbook of Social Psychology, ed. Daniel T. Gilbert, Susan T. Fiske, and Gardner Lindzey, 4th ed. (London: Oxford University Press, 1988), 778–867. The extended quote is from.

190詹姆斯·马奇也提出了一个相关的观点：他在其著作《决策入门》 （纽约：自由出版社，1994）的第一章和第二章中描述了两种决策模式——结果导向型和身份导向型。经济分析尤其假定所有决策都基于结果，因此在许多身份导向型决策至关重要的领域，经济分析会做出错误的预测；大多数经济学家都会惊讶于“别惹德克萨斯”运动如果不对乱扔垃圾的行为处以罚款就能奏效。

190 A related idea comes from James March: James March describes the two patterns of making decisions—consequence versus identity—in Chapters 1 and 2 of James G. March, A Primer on Decision Making (New York: Free Press, 1994). Economic analysis, in particular, assumes that all decisions are made on the basis of consequences, so it makes incorrect predictions in a number of arenas where identity is important; most economists would be surprised that the “Don’t Mess with Texas” campaign would work without imposing fines for littering.

在 1993 年的“代数”会议上：创意诊所中的第一条信息来自 Joseph G. Rosenstein、Janet H. Caldwell 和 Warren G. Crown，《新泽西州数学课程框架》（新泽西州：新泽西州教育部，1996 年）。

In a 1993 conference on “Algebra”: Message 1 in the Idea Clinic is from Joseph G. Rosenstein, Janet H. Caldwell, and Warren G. Crown, New Jersey Mathematics Curriculum Framework (New Jersey: New Jersey Department of Education, 1996).

消息 3：迪恩·谢尔曼的回应以及代数教师们对此问题的深入讨论可以在以下网址找到：http://mathforum.org/t2t/thread.taco? thread=1739 。

MESSAGE 3: Dean Sherman’s response and an extended discussion of this question among algebra teachers can be found at http://mathforum.org/t2t/thread.taco?thread=1739.

丹·西雷克是全国领先的：塞思·坎托，“不要招惹德克萨斯运动直接击中流氓乱扔垃圾者”，《奥斯汀美国政治家报》，1989 年 8 月 4 日，A1 版。

Dan Syrek is the nation’s leading: Seth Kantor, “Don’t Mess With Texas Campaign Scores Direct Hit with Ruffian Litterers,” Austin American-Statesman, August 4, 1989, A1.

“我们称他为巴布”：艾伦·斯通，《德克萨斯州的反乱扔垃圾运动进展顺利》，《旧金山纪事报》 ，1988 年 11 月 28 日，A4 版。

“We call him Bubba”: Allyn Stone, “The Anti-Litter Campaign in Texas Worked Just Fine,” San Francisco Chronicle, November 28, 1988, A4.

Too-Tall Jones 迈向：达拉斯牛仔队的这一举动在罗伯特·莱因霍尔德 (Robert Reinhold) 的文章《德克萨斯州正在打击乱扔垃圾者》中有所描述，该文章发表于 1986 年 12 月 14 日的《纽约时报》。

Too-Tall Jones steps toward: The Dallas Cowboys spot is described in Robert Reinhold, “Texas Is Taking a Swat at Litterbugs,” New York Times, December 14, 1986.

交通部原文： Marj Charlier，“像生活中的许多事情一样，路边垃圾也会根据季节进行调整”，《华尔街日报》，1989 年 8 月 3 日。

The Department of Transportation originally: Marj Charlier, “Like Much in Life, Roadside Refuse Is Seasonally Adjusted,” The Wall Street Journal, August 3, 1989.

6. 故事

6. Stories

护士正在工作：关于蓝黑色婴儿的故事可以在 Gary Klein 的《权力的来源：人们如何做出决定》（马萨诸塞州剑桥：麻省理工学院出版社，1998 年）第 178-179 页中找到。

The nurse was working: The story about the blue-black baby is found in Gary Klein, Sources of Power: How People Make Decisions (Cambridge, Mass.: MIT Press, 1998), 178–79.

故事能促使人们行动：与前几章一样，本章重点阐述了故事的一个优点——鼓励行动——但我们还可以探讨其他优点。故事还能帮助人们理解和记忆。抽象的故事难以讲述，因此故事继承了具体故事的所有优点，同时它们也以简洁（核心且紧凑）的方式整合了大量信息。关于陪审团决策的研究表明，陪审员在做出判决时非常依赖故事。陪审员面对的是大量事实，这些事实往往以杂乱无章的方式呈现。案件记录中存在大量信息缺失，且证人证词明显带有个人偏见，这种复杂的情况使得陪审员难以应对。他们如何应对？事实证明，他们会自发地构建一个或多个故事来解释这些纷繁的信息，然后将自己的故事与控辩双方的证词进行匹配，最终选择最符合自己想法的那一方。南希·彭宁顿和里德·哈斯蒂的一项研究表明，即使呈现的信息完全相同，陪审员能否轻易构建故事也会影响判决结果。当辩方按照事件发生的顺序呈现证据，而控方则没有按照事件发生的顺序呈现证据时，只有31%的陪审员投票认定被告有罪。而当呈现的信息完全相同，但辩方证人出庭作证的顺序与控方按照事件发生的顺序呈现证据时，78%的陪审员投票认定被告有罪。当双方都按照事件发生的顺序陈述证据时，陪审员对自己的决定最有信心。人们喜欢了解双方的说法，在脑海中清晰地看到证据，然后再做决定。参见 Nancy Pennington 和 Reid Hastie，“基于解释的决策：记忆结构对判断的影响”，《实验心理学杂志：学习、记忆与认知》 14 (1988): 521–33。

stories make people act: As in previous chapters, this chapter highlights one virtue of stories—encouraging action—but we could have discussed others. Stories also help people understand and remember. It’s hard to tell an abstract story, so stories inherit all the virtues of the Concrete, but they also serve as Simple (core and compact) ways of integrating lots of information. Research on jury decision-making shows that jurors rely heavily on stories to decide on their verdicts. Jurors confront masses of facts, presented in a scram bled sequence with substantial gaps in the record, filtered through the obvious personal biases of witnesses. How do they deal with this complexity? It turns out they spontaneously construct a story (or stories) to account for this welter of information, then match their personal story with the stories told by the prosecution and the defense and choose whichever side tells a story that best matches their own. In one study in this area, Nancy Pennington and Reid Hastie showed that verdicts shifted depending on how easily jurors were able to construct a story, even when identical information was presented. When the defense presented evidence in the order of an unfolding story but the prosecution presented evidence out of story order, only 31 percent of jurors voted to convict the defendant. When exactly the same information was presented but the defense presented witnesses out of order and the prosecution presented witnesses in story order, 78 percent of the jurors voted to convict. Jurors felt most confident in their decision when both sides presented in story order; people like to understand both stories, to see the evidence clearly in their mind, and then decide. See Nancy Pennington and Reid Hastie, “Explanation-based Decision Making: Effects of Memory Structure on Judgment,” Journal of Experimental Psychology: Learning, Memory & Cognition 14 (1988): 521–33.

故事也能提升可信度。研究人员梅兰妮·格林和蒂莫西·布罗克指出，直接经验形成的态度更具影响力，而故事能让我们感受到真实的体验。他们发现，当人们被故事“打动”——即更沉浸于故事的心理模拟中时，他们更容易被故事说服。参见梅兰妮·C·格林和蒂莫西·C·布罗克合著的《公众叙事说服力中的作用：沉浸感的作用》，载于《人格与社会心理学杂志》 79（2000）：701-21。

Stories also improve credibility. Researchers Melanie Green and Timothy Brock point out that attitudes formed by direct experience are more powerful, and stories give us the feeling of real experience. They show that people are more likely to be persuaded by a story when they are “transported” by it—when they feel more wrapped up in their mental simulation. See Melanie C. Green and Timothy C. Brock, “The Role of Transportation in the Persuasiveness of Public Narratives,” Journal of Personality and Social Psychology 79 (2000): 701–21.

新的 XER 板配置：朱利安·E·奥尔，《谈论机器：现代工作的民族志》（伊萨卡，纽约：康奈尔大学出版社，1996 年）。双极管的故事是……

The new XER board configuration: Julian E. Orr, Talking About Machines: An Ethnography of a Modern Job (Ithaca, N.Y.: Cornell University Press, 1996). The dicorotron story is.

“约翰 穿上了 他的运动衫”：这项研究是众多支持心理模拟重要性的研究之一。相关综述请参见 Rolf A. Zwaan 和 Gabriel A. Radvansky 合著的《语言理解和记忆中的情境模型》，载于《心理学公报》 123 (1998): 162–85。人们不仅会在心理上模拟空间，还会模拟时间。在一个关于人们进入电影院的故事中，如果故事只过了十分钟，受访者更容易认出“放映员”这个指代，而如果故事过了六个小时，即使这两个指代在页面上都与关于电影院的句子相隔不远。

“John put on his sweatshirt”: This study is among dozens of studies that support the importance of mental simulation. For a review, see Rolf A. Zwaan and Gabriel A. Radvansky, “Situation Models in Language Comprehension and Memory,” Psychological Bulletin 123 (1998): 162–85. Not only do people mentally simulate space, they also simulate time. In a story about people entering a movie theater, respondents are more likely to recognize a reference to “the projectionist” if only ten minutes have elapsed in the story than if six hours have elapsed, even if both references are just a few sentences away on the page from the line about the movie theater.

210根本不存在被动的读者：对“主动读者”研究的最佳概述来自该领域的研究者理查德·格里格（Richard Gerrig）。参见《体验叙事世界：论阅读的心理活动》（纽黑文，康涅狄格州：耶鲁大学出版社，1988）。格里格认为，塞缪尔·柯勒律治将我们欣赏故事的能力描述为“暂停怀疑”是错误的，因为他的引述暗示人类的默认状态是怀疑的、不相信的。事实上，真实情况恰恰相反。人们很容易沉浸在故事中；很难以怀疑的态度评估论点，在它们被证明之前保持怀疑。格里格对结局众所周知的故事的研究，是我们最喜欢的模拟力量的例证之一。当人们沉浸在故事中时，他们常常会完全沉浸在模拟中，以至于暂时忘记了显而易见的结局。小心冰山，泰坦尼克号！加州大学洛杉矶分校的研究由 Inna D. Rivkin 和 Shelley E. Taylor 完成，题为“心理模拟对应对可控压力事件的影响”，发表于《人格与社会心理学公报》 25 (1999): 1451–62。

210 no such thing as a passive audience: The best overview of the “active reader” research is provided by Richard Gerrig, a researcher in the field. See Experiencing Narrative Worlds: On the Psychological Activities of Reading (New Haven, Conn.: Yale University Press, 1988). Gerrig says that Samuel Coleridge was wrong to describe our ability to appreciate stories as the “suspension of disbelief,” because his quote implies that the default state of humans is skeptical disbelief. In fact, the real state is the opposite. It’s easy to get wrapped up in a story; it’s hard to evaluate arguments skeptically, disbelieving them until they are proven. One of our favorite illustrations of the power of simulation is Gerrig’s research on stories with well-known endings. When people are in the middle of a story, they often get so wrapped up in the simulation that they momentarily act as though they’d forgotten an obvious ending. Watch out for that iceberg, Titanic! The UCLA study is by Inna D. Rivkin and Shelley E. Taylor, “The Effects of Mental Simulation on Coping with Controllable Stressful Events,” Personality and Social Psychology Bulletin 25 (1999): 1451–62.

为什么心理模拟有效？：敲击、埃菲尔铁塔、柠檬汁和其他例子来自 Mark R. Dadds、Dana H. Bovbejerg、William H. Redd 和 Tim RH Cutmore 的“人类经典条件反射中的意象”，《心理学公报》 122 (1997): 89–103。

Why does mental simulation work?: The tapping, Eiffel Tower, lemon juice, and other examples are from Mark R. Dadds, Dana H. Bovbejerg, William H. Redd, and Tim R. H. Cutmore, “Imagery in Human Classical Conditioning,” Psychological Bulletin 122 (1997): 89–103.

对 35 项研究的回顾： James E. Driskell、Carolyn Copper 和 Aidan Moran，“心理练习能否提高表现？” 《应用心理学杂志》 79 (1994): 481–92。

A review of thirty-five studies: James E. Driskell, Carolyn Copper, and Aidan Moran, “Does Mental Practice Enhance Performance?” Journal of Applied Psychology 79 (1994): 481–92.

处理问题学生：信息 1 摘自印第安纳大学-普渡大学印第安纳波利斯分校专业发展办公室的提示单“处理学生问题行为的技巧”。请访问www.opd.iupui.edu/uploads/library/IDD/IDD6355.doc。信息 2 由 Alison Buckman 撰写，最初发布于http://research.umbc.edu/~korenman/wmst/disruptive_students2.html。

Dealing with Problem Students: Message 1 is from a tip sheet, “Tips for Dealing with Student Problem Behaviors,” from the Office for Professional Development, Indiana University–Purdue University Indianapolis. See www.opd.iupui.edu/uploads/library/IDD/IDD6355.doc. Message 2, by Alison Buckman, was originally posted to http://research.umbc.edu/~korenman/wmst/disruptive_students2.html.

Fogle 注册时： Ryan Coleman，“印第安纳大学大四学生获得新的人生视角”，《印第安纳日报学生版》，1999 年 4 月 29 日。Coad 的引语来自 David Kaplan，“一项失败的提议：Jared 为赛百味店名赋予了面孔”，《休斯顿纪事报》，2002 年 1 月 23 日，D1 版。

When Fogle registered: Ryan Coleman, “Indiana U. Senior Gains New Perspective on Life,” Indiana Daily Student, April 29, 1999. The Coad quotes are from David Kaplan, “A Losing Proposition: Jared Puts a Face to a Name for Subway Shops,” Houston Chronicle, January 23, 2002, D1.

1999 年赛百味的销售额：赛百味、Schlotzky's 和 Quiznos 的业绩统计数据来自 Bob Sperber，“寻找新鲜创意”，《品牌周刊》 ，2001 年 10 月 15 日，M54。

In 1999, Subway’s sales: Performance statistics for Subway, Schlotzky’s, and Quiznos are from Bob Sperber, “In Search of Fresh Ideas,” Brandweek, October 15, 2001, M54.

布鲁姆金是一位俄罗斯女性：沃伦·巴菲特在 1983 年致股东信中对罗斯·布鲁姆金进行了描述（参见www.berkshirehathaway.com/letters/1983.html）。

Blumkin is a Russian woman: Rose Blumkin is described by Warren Buffett in his 1983 shareholder letter (see www.berkshirehathaway.com/letters/1983.html).

226这三种基本情节：这些结果来自斯坦福大学奇普的研究。在研究了一段时间的都市传说——这类故事通常专门制造恐惧、愤怒或厌恶等负面情绪——之后，他提出疑问：是否存在一些故事因为能引发积极情绪而广为流传？《心灵鸡汤》系列故事显然是研究的起点。为了研究这三种情节出现的频率，研究人员向评分者提供了分类系统，但没有提供关于研究假设的其他信息。尽管评分者独立工作，但阅读相同故事的评分者之间在分类上表现出高度一致性。

226 These three basic plots: These results are from Chip’s research at Stanford. After studying urban legends for a while—stories that frequently specialize in creating negative emotions such as fear, anger, or disgust—he asked whether there were stories that circulated because they produced positive emotions. The Chicken Soup for the Soul stories were the obvious place to start. The research on the frequencies of the three plots was done by giving raters the classification system but no other information about the hypotheses of the research. Even though raters worked independently, pairs of raters who saw the same stories showed strong agreement on classifications.

斯坦福大学的另一项研究表明，这三种情节模式能有效提升故事的感染力。学生们被要求寻找一个能激励同学的真实故事，故事可以来自他们自己的生活经历，也可以来自公开渠道。之后，同学们根据故事的激励效果——即是否能让他们感到自豪、兴奋和坚定——对这些故事进行评分。结果显示，包含这三种情节模式之一的故事更有可能进入评分前50%。在排名前10%的故事中，所有故事都包含一种或多种这三种情节模式。

Another Stanford study suggests that these three plots are a good way to ensure that your stories are more inspiring. Students were given the assignment of finding a true story that would inspire their classmates, either from their own lives or from some public source. Later, their fellow students rated the stories on their ability to inspire—to make them feel proud, excited, and determined. Stories that featured one of the three plots were much more likely to wind up in the top half of the stories. Of the stories that wound up in the top 10 percent, all had one or more of the three plots.

另一项研究表明，每种情节都能激发特定的行为。参与者每天阅读一个故事，并记录阅读后的感受。挑战类情节促使人们设定更高的目标，迎接新的挑战，更加努力工作，坚持更长时间。联结类情节促使人们与他人合作，主动帮助他人，并更加宽容。创造力类情节促使人们尝试不同的事物，发挥创造力，尝试新的方法。因此，合适的故事不仅告诉我们应该如何行动，它们还像一个心理能量电池，赋予我们行动的能量。值得注意的是，这些故事都不是那种“感觉良好”的行为。人们并非更倾向于做一些让自己愉悦的事情——比如听音乐、看电视或享用美食；相反，他们更想走出去，完成一些事情。因此，这些故事激发的是积极的行动，而非被动的自我沉溺。

Another study showed that each plot drives a specific form of activity. People read through a selection of stories, one per day, and recorded how they felt after reading each story. Challenge plots made people want to set higher goals, to take on new challenges, to work harder and persist longer. Connection plots made people want to work with others, to reach out and help them, and to be more tolerant. Creativity plots made people want to do something different, to be creative, to experiment with new approaches. Thus, the right stories not only tell us how to act, they operate as a kind of psychological battery pack, giving us the energy to take action. Notably, none of these stories was more likely to drive “feel good” activity. People weren’t more likely to was to do something to enjoy themselves—to listen to good music, watch television, or eat a good meal; instead, they wanted to go out and accomplish something. Thus these stories drive productive action, not passive self-involvement.

耶稣就讲了一个故事来回应：好撒玛利亚人的故事出自《圣经新国际版》路加福音 10:25-37。

In response, Jesus told a story: The Good Samaritan story is from the New International Version of the Bible, Luke 10:25–37.

英格索兰： Grinder 团队的故事在汤姆·彼得斯所著的《解放管理》（纽约：克诺夫出版社，1992 年）第 6 章中有所讲述。

Ingersoll-Rand: The story of the Grinder Team is told in Chapter 6 of Tom Peters, Liberation Management (New York: Knopf, 1992).

世界银行的故事：本部分内容基于斯蒂芬·丹宁的两本优秀著作。他的第一本书《跳板：故事如何在知识时代激发行动》探讨了讲故事在组织中的作用。 组织（波士顿：Butterworth-Heinemann出版社，2001年）。市面上有很多书籍探讨故事在组织和组织文化中的作用；《跳板》是该主题的最佳著作，也是所有商业书籍中最优秀的作品之一。另一本后续著作《领导者故事指南：掌握商业叙事的艺术与技巧》（旧金山：Josey-Bass出版社，2005年）描述了除跳板故事之外的多种故事情节。“企业西伯利亚”的引语出自斯蒂芬·丹宁于2005年6月9日在IDEO的演讲。

Stories at the World Bank: This section is based on two excellent books by Stephen Denning. His first book, about the role of storytelling in organizations, is The Springboard: How Storytelling Ignites Action in Knowledge-Era Organizations (Boston: Butterworth-Heinemann, 2001). There are dozens of books available that talk about the role of storytelling in organizations and organizational culture; The Springboard is the best book on the subject, and among the best business books of any kind. A follow-up book that describes a number of story plots other than springboard stories is The Leader’s Guide to Storytelling: Mastering the Art and Discipline of Business Narrative (San Francisco: Josey-Bass, 2005), The “corporate Siberia” quote is from a talk by Stephen Denning at IDEO, June 9, 2005.

“这是一场非常奇怪的对话”：丹宁，《领导者指南》，第 63 页。

“This is a very strange conversation”: Denning, The Leader’s Guide, 63.

“为什么不把信息说清楚呢？”：丹宁，《跳板》，80。

“Why not spell out the message”: Denning, The Springboard, 80.

“内心深处的声音”：丹宁，《领导者指南》，第 62 页。

“little voice inside the head”: Denning, The Leader’s Guide, 62.

克莱因讲述了另一个故事：会议总结失败的故事出自克莱因的《权力之源》第 195-96 页。

Klein tells another story: The story of the failed conference summaries is from Klein, Sources of Power, 195–96.

尾声：哪些事能真正留下来

Epilogue: What Sticks

正如拉尔夫·凯斯在其著作《好人终有第七名：谬误的短语、伪造的谚语和常见的误引》（纽约：哈珀柯林斯出版社，1992年）中所述，这本书充满了谚语在社会传播过程中如何演变和变化的有趣例子。

As recounted by Ralph Keyes: Ralph Keyes, Nice Guys Finish Seventh: False Phrases, Spurious Sayings and Familiar Misquotations (New York: HarperCollins, 1992). This book is filled with interesting examples of how proverbs evolve and change as they spread in society.

许多人对我们的初步提案提出了反馈意见，该提案需要大量改进。我们感谢以下人士帮助我们撰写出一份足以通过第一轮关键测试的文件：Doug Crandall、James Dailey、Ben Ellis、John Lin、Tom Prehn、Chloe Sladden 和 Craig Yee。

Several people gave feedback on our initial proposal, which needed a lot of work. We thank the following people for helping us create a document that was compelling enough to pass the first essential test: Doug Crandall, James Dailey, Ben Ellis, John Lin, Tom Prehn, Chloe Sladden, and Craig Yee.

完成本书前半部分后，我们既兴奋又急切地想要得到一些反馈。于是我们把它发给了许多朋友和同事，他们很配合地给了我们一些书面反馈。他们也让读者们免受了许多不可信、无趣的轶事之苦，比如关于手链笑话的那个，所以你们也应该感谢他们。以下是所有好心人的名单：Daryl Anderson、E. Joseph Arias、Deena Bahri、Amy Bryant、Mark 和 Chelsea Dinsmore、Julie Balovich、Danny Fitelson、Alfred Edmond、Michael Erisman、Chris Ertel、Erika Faust、Craig Fox、Emmet Gaffney、Lisa Gansky、Liz Gerber、Julio Gonzalez、Eric Guenther、Steven Guerrero、Susanna Hamner 和 Byron Penstock、Tod 和 Susan Hays、Fred 和 Brenda Heath、Ian Hill、Joe Lassiter、Alex Kazaks、Brian Kelly、Paul Marshall、George Miller、Shara Morales、Michael Morris、Derek Newton、Justin Osofsky、Jeff Pfeffer、Bill Sahlman、Andrew 和 Katie Solomon、Melissa Studzin滑雪爱好者们，Mark Schlueter、Paul Schumann、Steven Slon、Amy 和 Walter Surdacki、Bob Sutton、Mike Sweeney、Anthony Trendl、Ed Uyeshima、Steve 和 Trae Vassallo、Rachel Ward、Keith Yamashita。感谢大家——你们在最需要的时候给予了我们宝贵的反馈。

When we finished the first half of the book, we were very excited and anxious to get some feedback. So we sent it to a bunch of friends and colleagues, who humored us and sent along written feedback. They also saved you, the reader, from lots of unconvincing and uninteresting anecdotes, like the one about the Charm Bracelet Punchline, so you owe them some gratitude as well. Here is the roll call of good people: Daryl Anderson, E. Joseph Arias, Deena Bahri, Amy Bryant, Mark and Chelsea Dinsmore, Julie Balovich, Danny Fitelson, Alfred Edmond, Michael Erisman, Chris Ertel, Erika Faust, Craig Fox, Emmet Gaffney, Lisa Gansky, Liz Gerber, Julio Gonzalez, Eric Guenther, Steven Guerrero, Susanna Hamner and Byron Penstock, Tod and Susan Hays, Fred and Brenda Heath, Ian Hill, Joe Lassiter, Alex Kazaks, Brian Kelly, Paul Marshall, George Miller, Shara Morales, Michael Morris, Derek Newton, Justin Osofsky, Jeff Pfeffer, Bill Sahlman, Andrew and Katie Solomon, Melissa Studzin ski, Mark Schlueter, Paul Schumann, Steven Slon, Amy and Walter Surdacki, Bob Sutton, Mike Sweeney, Anthony Trendl, Ed Uyeshima, Steve and Trae Vassallo, Rachel Ward, Keith Yamashita. Thank you all—you gave us the feedback we needed when it was most useful.

在此，我要向所有以独特方式帮助过我们的人致谢。感谢Noah Weiss，他在早期阶段花费数小时搜寻晦涩难懂的资料，寻找有趣的案例；感谢Maggie Cong-Huyen，她接手了他的工作。特别感谢Jeff Saunders指出了指挥官意图。感谢Chip多年来在OB 368课程中的学生们，他们帮助我们完善和测试了这个框架。感谢Chip的合作者们，他们帮助我们完善了许多想法：Adrian Bangerter、Chris Bell、Jonah Berger、Sanford Devoe、Nate Fast、Alison Fragale、Emily Sternberg和Scott Wiltermuth。感谢以下人士阅读本书并帮助收集国际版案例：Eugine Chong、Hide Doi、Atsuko Jenks、Hyun Kim、Motoki Korenaga、Andreas Kornstaedt和Noriko Masuda。

Here is a hodgepodge of thank yous for people who helped us in distinctive ways. Thank you to Noah Weiss, who spent hours combing obsure sources for interesting examples in the early stages, and to Maggie Cong-Huyen, who picked up where he left off. Special thanks to Jeff Saunders for pointing out Commander’s Intent. Thanks to Chip’s students in several years of OB 368 classes, who helped refine and test this framework. Thanks to Chip’s collaborators who helped work out many of these ideas: Adrian Bangerter, Chris Bell, Jonah Berger, Sanford Devoe, Nate Fast, Alison Fragale, Emily Sternberg, Scott Wiltermuth. Thanks to the following people who read the book and helped collect examples for the international editions: Eugine Chong, Hide Doi, Atsuko Jenks, Hyun Kim, Motoki Korenaga, Andreas Kornstaedt, and Noriko Masuda.

接下来是那些作品启发了我们的人的段落。你们与本书并无直接关联，但你们的贡献却对本书产生了深远的影响。谨以此文向以下人士致敬：爱德华·塔夫特、唐·诺曼、马尔科姆·格拉德威尔、詹姆斯·卡维尔、斯蒂芬·丹宁、罗伯特·麦基、安迪·古德曼、吉姆·汤普森、史蒂文·汤姆林森、爱德华·伯格、乔治·沃尔夫、大卫·普莱塞克、基思·山下、雅各布·戈尔登伯格、乔治·洛温斯坦、罗伯特·西奥迪尼、马克·沙勒、大卫·鲁宾、扬·布伦万德，以及其他许多我们因未能及时列入名单而感到遗憾的人。

Now for the paragraph of people whose work inspired us. You didn’t have anything to do with this book, and yet you had a lot to do with this book. A fan letter to: Edward Tufte, Don Norman, Malcolm Gladwell, James Carville, Stephen Denning, Robert McKee, Andy Goodman, Jim Thompson, Steven Tomlinson, Edward Burger, George Wolfe, David Placek, Keith Yamashita, Jacob Goldenberg, George Loewenstein, Robert Cialdini, Mark Schaller, David Rubin, Jan Brunvand, and many others that we’re going to kick ourselves for not including by press time.

我们衷心感谢我们的经纪人唐·拉姆和克里斯蒂·弗莱彻，是你们让这次合作成为可能。同时感谢马克·福蒂埃，感谢他为宣传这本书所做的一切。

To our agents Don Lamm and Christy Fletcher, we are deeply grateful to you for making this experience possible. And thank you to Mark Fortier, for spreading the word about the book.

感谢出版社是必须的。但据我们从其他作者那里了解到的情况，你通常得咬牙切齿地说声谢谢。这是我们的第一本书，在出版过程中，我们一直在努力让它达到市场标准。凯特，我们原本以为会经历很多出版业的糟心事。我们原本以为会感到不被赏识，得不到应有的支持。兰登书屋在这方面彻底失败了。然而，兰登书屋的同事们却让我感到无比愉快，他们出乎意料地热情友好，以至于我都不知道该从何说起。感谢：黛比·阿罗夫、阿维德·巴希拉德、​​瑞秋·伯恩斯坦、妮可·邦德、埃文·坎菲尔德、吉娜·森特雷洛、克里斯汀·法斯勒、詹妮弗·赫尔希、斯蒂芬妮·亨特沃克、詹妮弗·胡尔、伦敦·金、莎莉·马文、丹·梅纳克、杰克·佩里、汤姆·佩里、凯莉·鲁登、罗宾·希夫和卡罗尔·施耐德。还有桑尤·狄龙，我的天哪，我们该如何才能充分表达对你所做的一切的感激之情呢？简直难以置信。

It is obligatory to thank your publisher. According to what we’ve heard from other authors, you usually have to say thanks through gritted teeth. This is our first book, and in the process of getting it to mar ket, we were supposed to accumulate a lot of publishing horror stories. We were supposed to feel underappreciated and undersupported. Random House has been an utter failure on this front. The people at Random House have been such a joy to work with, so unexpectedly and unnecessarily nice, that it’s hard to know where to begin. Thanks to: Debbie Aroff, Avideh Bashirrad, Rachel Bernstein, Nicole Bond, Evan Camfield, Gina Centrello, Kristin Fassler, Jennifer Hershey, Stephanie Huntwork, Jennifer Huwer, London King, Sally Marvin, Dan Menaker, Jack Perry, Tom Perry, Kelle Ruden, Robbin Schiff, and Carol Schneider. And Sanyu Dillon, my goodness, how can we adequately express our appreciation for all that you’ve done? Unbelievable.

还有，我们要用书法手写一份巨大的、镌刻的、烫金的、镌刻的感谢，献给我们了不起的编辑本·洛恩。如果幸运的话，我们的父母会读一遍这本书。（顺便提一句：我爸有一套文学数量论，他认为少于四百页的书不值得读。我们只能靠关系让他读一遍了。）与此同时，本可能已经读了这本书十几遍了，却从未崩溃过，至少在我们面前是这样。本，你真是太有耐心、太体贴、太有洞察力、太支持我们了。好吧，这听起来好像我们在给你的毕业纪念册签名。（别紧张！）但说真的，我们知道有时候我们让你很难接受。比如我们那次用胶带封住书的行动。

And a huge, engraved, foil-embossed, calligraphied thank you to our amazing editor, Ben Loehnen. Our parents will read this book once, if we’re lucky. (An aside: Our dad has a Quantity Theory of Literature, which says that it’s not worth reading a book with fewer than four hundred pages. We’re relying purely on connections to get a read out of him.) Meanwhile, Ben has read this thing probably a dozen times without ever breaking down, at least in front of us. Ben, you are endlessly patient, thoughtful, insightful, and supportive. Okay, this is starting to sound like we’re signing your yearbook. (Stay cool!) But, really, we know we gave you a lot to stomach at times. Like our duct tape campaign.

现在，我要感谢我们的家人，感谢你们包容我们。我们由衷地感谢你们，这并非因为这本书，而是因为你们一直以来对我们的支持。这部分内容只是让我们有机会用文字表达我们的感激之情。谢谢爸爸妈妈，感谢你们给了我们这些合作者。谢谢苏珊。谢谢埃默里。谢谢苏珊姐姐。谢谢托德、亨特和达比。

Now to our family, who put up with us. We owe you this heartfelt thank you, which really has nothing to do with this book and everything to do with your support of us in all that we do. This section just gives us a great opportunity to put it in black and white. Thanks, Mom and Dad, for giving us co-authors. Thanks, Susan. Thanks, Emory. Thanks, sister Susan. Thanks, Tod, Hunter, and Darby.

希望您喜欢这本书。我们写作的过程非常愉快。

We hope you enjoy the book. We had fun writing it.

关于作者

ABOUT THE AUTHORS

奇普·希思是斯坦福大学商学院组织行为学教授，现居加利福尼亚州洛斯加托斯。

CHIP HEATH is a professor of organizational behavior in the Graduate School of Business at Stanford University. He lives in Los Gatos, California.

丹·希思是阿斯彭研究所政策项目的顾问。他曾是哈佛商学院的研究员，也是创新型新媒体教科书公司Thinkwell的联合创始人。他现居北卡罗来纳州罗利市。

DAN HEATH is a consultant to the Policy Programs at the Aspen Institute. A former researcher at Harvard Business School, he is a co-founder of Thinkwell, an innovative new-media textbook company. He lives in Raleigh, North Carolina.
